\"\u003E[47] That also follows from a series of decisions that say that the application of the laws of the various Australian Parliaments to Indigenous Australians does not depend on the acquiescence or consent of those people: see \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586040\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600591\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-invalid\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67871\"\u003EWalker v New South Wales\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [1994] HCA 64; (1994) 182 CLR 45 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586039\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-invalid\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67871/section/2616\"\u003E48-9\u003C/A\u003E per Mason CJ\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600591_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586045\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600592\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164\"\u003EMcDonald v DPP\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (2010) 26 VR 242 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586042\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/626\"\u003E[6]\u003C/A\u003E and \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586043\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/140124\"\u003E[16]\u003C/A\u003E per Ashley JA and \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586044\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/1398\"\u003E[191]\u003C/A\u003E per Neave JA, Redlich JA\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600592_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E agreeing; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586049\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600593\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/168511\"\u003EJones v Public Trustee (Qld)\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [2004] QCA 269; (2004) 209 ALR 106 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586048\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/168511/section/5399\"\u003E[14]-[15]\u003C/A\u003E per McPherson JA, Williams and Jerrard JJA\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600593_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E agreeing; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586053\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600594\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/256102\"\u003ER v Buzzacott\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [2004] ACTSC 89; (2004) 154 ACTR 37 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586052\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/256102/section/45774\"\u003E[3]-[17]\u003C/A\u003E per Connolly J\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600594_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E.”\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[10]/BLOCKQUOTE/P/I[1]/TEXT()","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[10]/BLOCKQUOTE/P/I[1]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL start\u003D\"35\"\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"37\"\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_sr_140276\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CP\u003EThe primary judge’s rejection of the applicant’s contentions was consistent with the decision of the High Court in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586014\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600581\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600581_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E. True it is, as the applicant submitted, that the High Court rejected the doctrine of terra nullius in that case. However, the Court also rejected a concept of Indigenous sovereignty adverse to the Crown. At first instance in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586018\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600582\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/956885\"\u003EIndigenous Land and Sea Corporation v Anderson \u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E[2022] NSWSC 1650; 406 ALR 484 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586017\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/956885/section/2883\"\u003E[32]-[34]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600582_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, Griffiths AJ referred with approval to the reasoning of Mansfield J in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586022\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600583\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/298938\"\u003EWalker v South Australia (No 2) \u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E(2013) 215 FCR 254; [2013] FCA 700 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586021\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/298938/section/17272\"\u003E[43]-[47]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600583_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, where his Honour stated: \u003C/P\u003E \u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E“[43] The contention that the Indigenous peoples of Australia constitute a sovereign nation or nations, has been expressly rejected. In \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586026\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600584\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/188346\"\u003ECoe v Commonwealth\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [1979] HCA 68; (1979) 53 ALJR 403 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586025\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/188346/section/140102\"\u003E408\u003C/A\u003E,\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600584_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E Gibbs J, with whom Aicken J agreed, held:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003EThe aboriginal people are subject to the laws of the Commonwealth and of the States or Territories in which they respectively reside. They have no legislative, executive or judicial organs by which sovereignty might be exercised. If such organs existed, they would have no powers, except such as the laws of the Commonwealth, or of a State or Territory, might confer upon them. The contention that there is in Australia an aboriginal nation exercising sovereignty, even of a limited kind, is quite impossible in law to maintain.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[44] That is consistent with \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586027\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600585\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600585_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E. The rights recognised with respect to native title in \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586028\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600586\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2) \u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600586_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003Eare, at common law, subject to statutory modification: see at 110–111 per Deane and Gaudron JJ:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003ELike other legal rights, including rights of property, the rights conferred by common law native title and the title itself can be dealt with, expropriated or extinguished by valid Commonwealth, State or Territorial legislation operating within the State or Territory in which the land in question is situated. To put the matter differently, the rights are not entrenched in the sense that they are, by reason of their nature, beyond the reach of legislative power.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[45] \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586030\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600587\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E reveals that, upon the settlement in New South Wales, and by extension later in South Australia, the English settlers brought with them the law of England, so that, at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586029\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140624\"\u003E38\u003C/A\u003E per Brennan J\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600587_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[t]he common law thus became the common law of all subjects within the Colony who were equally entitled to the law’s protection as subjects of the Crown. ... Thus the Meriam people in 1879, like Australian Aborigines in earlier times, became British subjects owing allegiance to the Imperial Sovereign entitled to such rights and privileges and subject to such liabilities as the common law and applicable statutes provided.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[46] Mason CJ in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586034\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600588\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67773\"\u003ECoe v Commonwealth\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [1993] HCA 42; (1993) 68 ALJR 110 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586033\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67773/section/140874\"\u003E116\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600588_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E confirmed that \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586035\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600589\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600589_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E is inconsistent with the notion of sovereignty in the Aboriginal people of Australia:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586036\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600590\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600590_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E is entirely at odds with the notion that sovereignty adverse to the Crown resides in the Aboriginal people of Australia. The decision is equally at odds with the notion that there resides in the Aboriginal people a limited kind of sovereignty embraced in the notion that they are “a domestic dependent nation” entitled to self-government and full rights (save the right of alienation) or that as a free and independent people they are entitled to any rights and interests other than those created or recognised by the laws of the Commonwealth, the State of New South Wales and the common law.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[47] That also follows from a series of decisions that say that the application of the laws of the various Australian Parliaments to Indigenous Australians does not depend on the acquiescence or consent of those people: see \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586040\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600591\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-invalid\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67871\"\u003EWalker v New South Wales\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [1994] HCA 64; (1994) 182 CLR 45 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586039\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-invalid\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67871/section/2616\"\u003E48-9\u003C/A\u003E per Mason CJ\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600591_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586045\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600592\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164\"\u003EMcDonald v DPP\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (2010) 26 VR 242 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586042\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/626\"\u003E[6]\u003C/A\u003E and \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586043\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/140124\"\u003E[16]\u003C/A\u003E per Ashley JA and \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586044\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/1398\"\u003E[191]\u003C/A\u003E per Neave JA, Redlich JA\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600592_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E agreeing; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586049\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600593\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/168511\"\u003EJones v Public Trustee (Qld)\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [2004] QCA 269; (2004) 209 ALR 106 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586048\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/168511/section/5399\"\u003E[14]-[15]\u003C/A\u003E per McPherson JA, Williams and Jerrard JJA\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600593_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E agreeing; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586053\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600594\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/256102\"\u003ER v Buzzacott\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [2004] ACTSC 89; (2004) 154 ACTR 37 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586052\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/256102/section/45774\"\u003E[3]-[17]\u003C/A\u003E per Connolly J\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600594_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E.”\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[12]/BLOCKQUOTE/P/I[2]/TEXT()","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[12]/BLOCKQUOTE/P/I[2]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL start\u003D\"35\"\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"37\"\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_sr_140276\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CP\u003EThe primary judge’s rejection of the applicant’s contentions was consistent with the decision of the High Court in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586014\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600581\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600581_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E. True it is, as the applicant submitted, that the High Court rejected the doctrine of terra nullius in that case. However, the Court also rejected a concept of Indigenous sovereignty adverse to the Crown. At first instance in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586018\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600582\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/956885\"\u003EIndigenous Land and Sea Corporation v Anderson \u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E[2022] NSWSC 1650; 406 ALR 484 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586017\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/956885/section/2883\"\u003E[32]-[34]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600582_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, Griffiths AJ referred with approval to the reasoning of Mansfield J in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586022\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600583\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/298938\"\u003EWalker v South Australia (No 2) \u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E(2013) 215 FCR 254; [2013] FCA 700 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586021\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/298938/section/17272\"\u003E[43]-[47]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600583_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, where his Honour stated: \u003C/P\u003E \u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E“[43] The contention that the Indigenous peoples of Australia constitute a sovereign nation or nations, has been expressly rejected. In \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586026\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600584\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/188346\"\u003ECoe v Commonwealth\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [1979] HCA 68; (1979) 53 ALJR 403 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586025\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/188346/section/140102\"\u003E408\u003C/A\u003E,\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600584_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E Gibbs J, with whom Aicken J agreed, held:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003EThe aboriginal people are subject to the laws of the Commonwealth and of the States or Territories in which they respectively reside. They have no legislative, executive or judicial organs by which sovereignty might be exercised. If such organs existed, they would have no powers, except such as the laws of the Commonwealth, or of a State or Territory, might confer upon them. The contention that there is in Australia an aboriginal nation exercising sovereignty, even of a limited kind, is quite impossible in law to maintain.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[44] That is consistent with \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586027\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600585\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600585_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E. The rights recognised with respect to native title in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586028\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600586\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2) \u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600586_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003Eare, at common law, subject to statutory modification: see at 110–111 per Deane and Gaudron JJ:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003ELike other legal rights, including rights of property, the rights conferred by common law native title and the title itself can be dealt with, expropriated or extinguished by valid Commonwealth, State or Territorial legislation operating within the State or Territory in which the land in question is situated. To put the matter differently, the rights are not entrenched in the sense that they are, by reason of their nature, beyond the reach of legislative power.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[45] \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586030\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600587\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E reveals that, upon the settlement in New South Wales, and by extension later in South Australia, the English settlers brought with them the law of England, so that, at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586029\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140624\"\u003E38\u003C/A\u003E per Brennan J\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600587_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[t]he common law thus became the common law of all subjects within the Colony who were equally entitled to the law’s protection as subjects of the Crown. ... Thus the Meriam people in 1879, like Australian Aborigines in earlier times, became British subjects owing allegiance to the Imperial Sovereign entitled to such rights and privileges and subject to such liabilities as the common law and applicable statutes provided.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[46] Mason CJ in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586034\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600588\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67773\"\u003ECoe v Commonwealth\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [1993] HCA 42; (1993) 68 ALJR 110 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586033\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67773/section/140874\"\u003E116\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600588_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E confirmed that \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586035\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600589\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600589_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E is inconsistent with the notion of sovereignty in the Aboriginal people of Australia:\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586036\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600590\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600590_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E is entirely at odds with the notion that sovereignty adverse to the Crown resides in the Aboriginal people of Australia. The decision is equally at odds with the notion that there resides in the Aboriginal people a limited kind of sovereignty embraced in the notion that they are “a domestic dependent nation” entitled to self-government and full rights (save the right of alienation) or that as a free and independent people they are entitled to any rights and interests other than those created or recognised by the laws of the Commonwealth, the State of New South Wales and the common law.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[47] That also follows from a series of decisions that say that the application of the laws of the various Australian Parliaments to Indigenous Australians does not depend on the acquiescence or consent of those people: see \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586040\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600591\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-invalid\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67871\"\u003EWalker v New South Wales\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [1994] HCA 64; (1994) 182 CLR 45 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586039\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-invalid\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67871/section/2616\"\u003E48-9\u003C/A\u003E per Mason CJ\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600591_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586045\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600592\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164\"\u003EMcDonald v DPP\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (2010) 26 VR 242 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586042\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/626\"\u003E[6]\u003C/A\u003E and \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586043\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/140124\"\u003E[16]\u003C/A\u003E per Ashley JA and \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586044\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/1398\"\u003E[191]\u003C/A\u003E per Neave JA, Redlich JA\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600592_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E agreeing; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586049\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600593\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/168511\"\u003EJones v Public Trustee (Qld)\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [2004] QCA 269; (2004) 209 ALR 106 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586048\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/168511/section/5399\"\u003E[14]-[15]\u003C/A\u003E per McPherson JA, Williams and Jerrard JJA\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600593_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E agreeing; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586053\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600594\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/256102\"\u003ER v Buzzacott\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [2004] ACTSC 89; (2004) 154 ACTR 37 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586052\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/256102/section/45774\"\u003E[3]-[17]\u003C/A\u003E per Connolly J\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600594_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E.”\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","Para 142","Para 141","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL[5]/LI[4]/P/TEXT()[3]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL[5]/LI[4]/P/I[2]","Para 25","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"25\"\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_sr_140706\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CP\u003EThe applicant also relied on \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872585990\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600566\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600566_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E and the rejection of terra nullius. His Honour stated that \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872585992\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600567\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo (No 2) \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003Ein fact demonstrated why the applicant’s arguments were futile. A key step in the Court’s reasoning was that native title is extinguished by a grant in fee, which includes a grant in fee simple: at \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872585991\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/5211\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E[141]-[142]\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600567_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E. His Honour stated that “the Plaintiff is entitled to possession [as] against all the world” in light of the registered title, and the applicant had not demonstrated otherwise: at\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600568\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872585993\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/1154474/section/1293\"\u003E[145]\u003C/A\u003E.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600568_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E \u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_e_t_sr_140706\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","Para 32","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL[4]/LI[2]/P/TEXT()[3]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL[4]/LI[2]/P/I[2]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"15\"\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_sr_140827\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CP\u003EThe applicant raised fraud in his oral submissions at the hearing. Although this was not something he had pleaded, the primary judge considered it as a matter of substance, noting the fraud exception in\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600541\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E s \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872585942\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/276819/section/94\"\u003E42\u003C/A\u003E of the \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872585943\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/276819\"\u003EReal Property Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600541_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E: at\u00A0[31].  The applicant contended that the Nyangbul people, the traditional custodians of the Land, had been defrauded of ownership by virtue of the conduct of European settlers in dispossessing them from the Land, referring to a want of self-determination for himself, the Nyangbul people and others, the want of a treaty or other agreement, and the rejection of terra nullius in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872585945\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600542\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E: at \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872585944\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/260\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E[32]\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600542_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E. In rejecting that contention, the primary judge stated that consistently with authority, for conduct to constitute fraud within the meaning of\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600543\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E s \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872585946\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/276819/section/94\"\u003E42\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600543_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E it must be directed at the specific act of registration, and nothing the applicant had advanced was capable of attracting the exception: at [34]-[35].\u003C/P\u003E \u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_e_t_sr_140827\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[11]/BLOCKQUOTE/P/TEXT()[2]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[11]/BLOCKQUOTE/P/I","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL start\u003D\"35\"\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"37\"\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_sr_140276\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CP\u003EThe primary judge’s rejection of the applicant’s contentions was consistent with the decision of the High Court in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586014\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600581\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600581_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E. True it is, as the applicant submitted, that the High Court rejected the doctrine of terra nullius in that case. However, the Court also rejected a concept of Indigenous sovereignty adverse to the Crown. At first instance in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586018\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600582\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/956885\"\u003EIndigenous Land and Sea Corporation v Anderson \u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E[2022] NSWSC 1650; 406 ALR 484 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586017\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/956885/section/2883\"\u003E[32]-[34]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600582_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, Griffiths AJ referred with approval to the reasoning of Mansfield J in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586022\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600583\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/298938\"\u003EWalker v South Australia (No 2) \u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E(2013) 215 FCR 254; [2013] FCA 700 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586021\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/298938/section/17272\"\u003E[43]-[47]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600583_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, where his Honour stated: \u003C/P\u003E \u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E“[43] The contention that the Indigenous peoples of Australia constitute a sovereign nation or nations, has been expressly rejected. In \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586026\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600584\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/188346\"\u003ECoe v Commonwealth\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [1979] HCA 68; (1979) 53 ALJR 403 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586025\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/188346/section/140102\"\u003E408\u003C/A\u003E,\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600584_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E Gibbs J, with whom Aicken J agreed, held:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003EThe aboriginal people are subject to the laws of the Commonwealth and of the States or Territories in which they respectively reside. They have no legislative, executive or judicial organs by which sovereignty might be exercised. If such organs existed, they would have no powers, except such as the laws of the Commonwealth, or of a State or Territory, might confer upon them. The contention that there is in Australia an aboriginal nation exercising sovereignty, even of a limited kind, is quite impossible in law to maintain.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[44] That is consistent with \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586027\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600585\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600585_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E. The rights recognised with respect to native title in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586028\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600586\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2) \u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600586_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003Eare, at common law, subject to statutory modification: see at 110–111 per Deane and Gaudron JJ:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003ELike other legal rights, including rights of property, the rights conferred by common law native title and the title itself can be dealt with, expropriated or extinguished by valid Commonwealth, State or Territorial legislation operating within the State or Territory in which the land in question is situated. To put the matter differently, the rights are not entrenched in the sense that they are, by reason of their nature, beyond the reach of legislative power.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[45] \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586030\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600587\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E reveals that, upon the settlement in New South Wales, and by extension later in South Australia, the English settlers brought with them the law of England, so that, at \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586029\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140624\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E38\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E per Brennan J\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600587_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E:\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[t]he common law thus became the common law of all subjects within the Colony who were equally entitled to the law’s protection as subjects of the Crown. ... Thus the Meriam people in 1879, like Australian Aborigines in earlier times, became British subjects owing allegiance to the Imperial Sovereign entitled to such rights and privileges and subject to such liabilities as the common law and applicable statutes provided.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[46] Mason CJ in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586034\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600588\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67773\"\u003ECoe v Commonwealth\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [1993] HCA 42; (1993) 68 ALJR 110 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586033\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67773/section/140874\"\u003E116\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600588_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E confirmed that \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586035\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600589\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600589_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E is inconsistent with the notion of sovereignty in the Aboriginal people of Australia:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586036\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600590\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600590_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E is entirely at odds with the notion that sovereignty adverse to the Crown resides in the Aboriginal people of Australia. The decision is equally at odds with the notion that there resides in the Aboriginal people a limited kind of sovereignty embraced in the notion that they are “a domestic dependent nation” entitled to self-government and full rights (save the right of alienation) or that as a free and independent people they are entitled to any rights and interests other than those created or recognised by the laws of the Commonwealth, the State of New South Wales and the common law.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[47] That also follows from a series of decisions that say that the application of the laws of the various Australian Parliaments to Indigenous Australians does not depend on the acquiescence or consent of those people: see \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586040\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600591\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-invalid\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67871\"\u003EWalker v New South Wales\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [1994] HCA 64; (1994) 182 CLR 45 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586039\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-invalid\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67871/section/2616\"\u003E48-9\u003C/A\u003E per Mason CJ\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600591_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586045\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600592\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164\"\u003EMcDonald v DPP\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (2010) 26 VR 242 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586042\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/626\"\u003E[6]\u003C/A\u003E and \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586043\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/140124\"\u003E[16]\u003C/A\u003E per Ashley JA and \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586044\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/1398\"\u003E[191]\u003C/A\u003E per Neave JA, Redlich JA\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600592_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E agreeing; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586049\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600593\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/168511\"\u003EJones v Public Trustee (Qld)\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [2004] QCA 269; (2004) 209 ALR 106 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586048\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/168511/section/5399\"\u003E[14]-[15]\u003C/A\u003E per McPherson JA, Williams and Jerrard JJA\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600593_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E agreeing; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586053\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600594\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/256102\"\u003ER v Buzzacott\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [2004] ACTSC 89; (2004) 154 ACTR 37 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586052\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/256102/section/45774\"\u003E[3]-[17]\u003C/A\u003E per Connolly J\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600594_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E.”\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[10]/BLOCKQUOTE/P/I[2]/TEXT()","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[10]/BLOCKQUOTE/P/I[2]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL start\u003D\"35\"\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"37\"\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_sr_140276\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CP\u003EThe primary judge’s rejection of the applicant’s contentions was consistent with the decision of the High Court in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586014\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600581\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600581_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E. True it is, as the applicant submitted, that the High Court rejected the doctrine of terra nullius in that case. However, the Court also rejected a concept of Indigenous sovereignty adverse to the Crown. At first instance in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586018\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600582\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/956885\"\u003EIndigenous Land and Sea Corporation v Anderson \u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E[2022] NSWSC 1650; 406 ALR 484 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586017\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/956885/section/2883\"\u003E[32]-[34]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600582_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, Griffiths AJ referred with approval to the reasoning of Mansfield J in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586022\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600583\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/298938\"\u003EWalker v South Australia (No 2) \u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E(2013) 215 FCR 254; [2013] FCA 700 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586021\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/298938/section/17272\"\u003E[43]-[47]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600583_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, where his Honour stated: \u003C/P\u003E \u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E“[43] The contention that the Indigenous peoples of Australia constitute a sovereign nation or nations, has been expressly rejected. In \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586026\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600584\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/188346\"\u003ECoe v Commonwealth\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [1979] HCA 68; (1979) 53 ALJR 403 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586025\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/188346/section/140102\"\u003E408\u003C/A\u003E,\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600584_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E Gibbs J, with whom Aicken J agreed, held:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003EThe aboriginal people are subject to the laws of the Commonwealth and of the States or Territories in which they respectively reside. They have no legislative, executive or judicial organs by which sovereignty might be exercised. If such organs existed, they would have no powers, except such as the laws of the Commonwealth, or of a State or Territory, might confer upon them. The contention that there is in Australia an aboriginal nation exercising sovereignty, even of a limited kind, is quite impossible in law to maintain.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[44] That is consistent with \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586027\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600585\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600585_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E. The rights recognised with respect to native title in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586028\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600586\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo (No 2) \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600586_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003Eare, at common law, subject to statutory modification: see at 110–111 per Deane and Gaudron JJ:\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003ELike other legal rights, including rights of property, the rights conferred by common law native title and the title itself can be dealt with, expropriated or extinguished by valid Commonwealth, State or Territorial legislation operating within the State or Territory in which the land in question is situated. To put the matter differently, the rights are not entrenched in the sense that they are, by reason of their nature, beyond the reach of legislative power.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[45] \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586030\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600587\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E reveals that, upon the settlement in New South Wales, and by extension later in South Australia, the English settlers brought with them the law of England, so that, at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586029\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140624\"\u003E38\u003C/A\u003E per Brennan J\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600587_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[t]he common law thus became the common law of all subjects within the Colony who were equally entitled to the law’s protection as subjects of the Crown. ... Thus the Meriam people in 1879, like Australian Aborigines in earlier times, became British subjects owing allegiance to the Imperial Sovereign entitled to such rights and privileges and subject to such liabilities as the common law and applicable statutes provided.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[46] Mason CJ in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586034\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600588\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67773\"\u003ECoe v Commonwealth\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [1993] HCA 42; (1993) 68 ALJR 110 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586033\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67773/section/140874\"\u003E116\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600588_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E confirmed that \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586035\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600589\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600589_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E is inconsistent with the notion of sovereignty in the Aboriginal people of Australia:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586036\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600590\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600590_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E is entirely at odds with the notion that sovereignty adverse to the Crown resides in the Aboriginal people of Australia. The decision is equally at odds with the notion that there resides in the Aboriginal people a limited kind of sovereignty embraced in the notion that they are “a domestic dependent nation” entitled to self-government and full rights (save the right of alienation) or that as a free and independent people they are entitled to any rights and interests other than those created or recognised by the laws of the Commonwealth, the State of New South Wales and the common law.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[47] That also follows from a series of decisions that say that the application of the laws of the various Australian Parliaments to Indigenous Australians does not depend on the acquiescence or consent of those people: see \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586040\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600591\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-invalid\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67871\"\u003EWalker v New South Wales\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [1994] HCA 64; (1994) 182 CLR 45 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586039\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-invalid\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67871/section/2616\"\u003E48-9\u003C/A\u003E per Mason CJ\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600591_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586045\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600592\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164\"\u003EMcDonald v DPP\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (2010) 26 VR 242 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586042\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/626\"\u003E[6]\u003C/A\u003E and \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586043\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/140124\"\u003E[16]\u003C/A\u003E per Ashley JA and \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586044\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/1398\"\u003E[191]\u003C/A\u003E per Neave JA, Redlich JA\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600592_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E agreeing; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586049\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600593\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/168511\"\u003EJones v Public Trustee (Qld)\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [2004] QCA 269; (2004) 209 ALR 106 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586048\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/168511/section/5399\"\u003E[14]-[15]\u003C/A\u003E per McPherson JA, Williams and Jerrard JJA\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600593_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E agreeing; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586053\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600594\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/256102\"\u003ER v Buzzacott\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [2004] ACTSC 89; (2004) 154 ACTR 37 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586052\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/256102/section/45774\"\u003E[3]-[17]\u003C/A\u003E per Connolly J\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600594_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E.”\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL[5]/LI[4]/P/I[1]/TEXT()","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL[5]/LI[4]/P/I[1]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"25\"\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_sr_140706\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CP\u003EThe applicant also relied on \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872585990\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600566\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600566_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E and the rejection of terra nullius. His Honour stated that \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872585992\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600567\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2) \u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003Ein fact demonstrated why the applicant’s arguments were futile. A key step in the Court’s reasoning was that native title is extinguished by a grant in fee, which includes a grant in fee simple: at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872585991\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/5211\"\u003E[141]-[142]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600567_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E. His Honour stated that “the Plaintiff is entitled to possession [as] against all the world” in light of the registered title, and the applicant had not demonstrated otherwise: at\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600568\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872585993\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/1154474/section/1293\"\u003E[145]\u003C/A\u003E.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600568_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E \u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_e_t_sr_140706\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL[9]/LI[3]/P/I[1]/TEXT()","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL[9]/LI[3]/P/I[1]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL start\u003D\"35\"\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"37\"\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_sr_140276\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CP\u003EThe primary judge’s rejection of the applicant’s contentions was consistent with the decision of the High Court in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586014\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600581\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600581_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E. True it is, as the applicant submitted, that the High Court rejected the doctrine of terra nullius in that case. However, the Court also rejected a concept of Indigenous sovereignty adverse to the Crown. At first instance in \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586018\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600582\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/956885\"\u003EIndigenous Land and Sea Corporation v Anderson \u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E[2022] NSWSC 1650; 406 ALR 484 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586017\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/956885/section/2883\"\u003E[32]-[34]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600582_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, Griffiths AJ referred with approval to the reasoning of Mansfield J in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586022\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600583\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/298938\"\u003EWalker v South Australia (No 2) \u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E(2013) 215 FCR 254; [2013] FCA 700 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586021\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/298938/section/17272\"\u003E[43]-[47]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600583_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, where his Honour stated: \u003C/P\u003E \u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E“[43] The contention that the Indigenous peoples of Australia constitute a sovereign nation or nations, has been expressly rejected. In \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586026\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600584\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/188346\"\u003ECoe v Commonwealth\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [1979] HCA 68; (1979) 53 ALJR 403 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586025\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/188346/section/140102\"\u003E408\u003C/A\u003E,\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600584_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E Gibbs J, with whom Aicken J agreed, held:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003EThe aboriginal people are subject to the laws of the Commonwealth and of the States or Territories in which they respectively reside. They have no legislative, executive or judicial organs by which sovereignty might be exercised. If such organs existed, they would have no powers, except such as the laws of the Commonwealth, or of a State or Territory, might confer upon them. The contention that there is in Australia an aboriginal nation exercising sovereignty, even of a limited kind, is quite impossible in law to maintain.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[44] That is consistent with \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586027\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600585\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600585_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E. The rights recognised with respect to native title in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586028\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600586\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2) \u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600586_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003Eare, at common law, subject to statutory modification: see at 110–111 per Deane and Gaudron JJ:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003ELike other legal rights, including rights of property, the rights conferred by common law native title and the title itself can be dealt with, expropriated or extinguished by valid Commonwealth, State or Territorial legislation operating within the State or Territory in which the land in question is situated. To put the matter differently, the rights are not entrenched in the sense that they are, by reason of their nature, beyond the reach of legislative power.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[45] \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586030\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600587\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E reveals that, upon the settlement in New South Wales, and by extension later in South Australia, the English settlers brought with them the law of England, so that, at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586029\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140624\"\u003E38\u003C/A\u003E per Brennan J\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600587_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[t]he common law thus became the common law of all subjects within the Colony who were equally entitled to the law’s protection as subjects of the Crown. ... Thus the Meriam people in 1879, like Australian Aborigines in earlier times, became British subjects owing allegiance to the Imperial Sovereign entitled to such rights and privileges and subject to such liabilities as the common law and applicable statutes provided.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[46] Mason CJ in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586034\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600588\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67773\"\u003ECoe v Commonwealth\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [1993] HCA 42; (1993) 68 ALJR 110 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586033\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67773/section/140874\"\u003E116\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600588_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E confirmed that \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586035\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600589\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600589_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E is inconsistent with the notion of sovereignty in the Aboriginal people of Australia:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586036\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600590\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600590_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E is entirely at odds with the notion that sovereignty adverse to the Crown resides in the Aboriginal people of Australia. The decision is equally at odds with the notion that there resides in the Aboriginal people a limited kind of sovereignty embraced in the notion that they are “a domestic dependent nation” entitled to self-government and full rights (save the right of alienation) or that as a free and independent people they are entitled to any rights and interests other than those created or recognised by the laws of the Commonwealth, the State of New South Wales and the common law.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:88%; \"\u003E[47] That also follows from a series of decisions that say that the application of the laws of the various Australian Parliaments to Indigenous Australians does not depend on the acquiescence or consent of those people: see \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586040\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600591\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-invalid\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67871\"\u003EWalker v New South Wales\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [1994] HCA 64; (1994) 182 CLR 45 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586039\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-invalid\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67871/section/2616\"\u003E48-9\u003C/A\u003E per Mason CJ\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600591_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586045\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600592\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164\"\u003EMcDonald v DPP\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (2010) 26 VR 242 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586042\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/626\"\u003E[6]\u003C/A\u003E and \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586043\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/140124\"\u003E[16]\u003C/A\u003E per Ashley JA and \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586044\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/139164/section/1398\"\u003E[191]\u003C/A\u003E per Neave JA, Redlich JA\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600592_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E agreeing; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586049\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600593\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/168511\"\u003EJones v Public Trustee (Qld)\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [2004] QCA 269; (2004) 209 ALR 106 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586048\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/168511/section/5399\"\u003E[14]-[15]\u003C/A\u003E per McPherson JA, Williams and Jerrard JJA\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600593_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E agreeing; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586053\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600594\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/256102\"\u003ER v Buzzacott\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [2004] ACTSC 89; (2004) 154 ACTR 37 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_cc_872586052\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/256102/section/45774\"\u003E[3]-[17]\u003C/A\u003E per Connolly J\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1154474_ct_603600594_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E.”\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","JAGOT J.","Mabo v Queensland [No 2]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[606]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()[2]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[606]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/I[2]","Para 271","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html;
