charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"244\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_sr_9460\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EPriestley\u00A0JA continued, saying:\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643930\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn300\" name\u003D\"_ftnref300\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[300]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643930_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\"The juxtaposition of par\u00A0(a) and par\u00A0(b) of\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643931\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E s\u00A0\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871129287\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/274763/section/1939\"\u003E36(1)\u003C/A\u003E of the \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871129288\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/274763\"\u003EAboriginal Land Rights Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643931_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E makes it clear that occupation in the foregoing broad sense [that is, \u0027the doctrine of acquisition of sovereignty of territory \"by occupation (or \u0027settlement\u0027 to use the term of the common law)\"\u0027\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643932\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn301\" name\u003D\"_ftnref301\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[301]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643932_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E] is not what par\u00A0(b) refers to or means. The word \u0027occupied\u0027 in par\u00A0(b) must have a more limited meaning. How should the limitation be described?\"\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_e_t_sr_9460\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref301\" name\u003D\"_ftn301\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[301]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-7\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871130390\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602644379\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/275300\"\u003EDaruk\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602644379_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871130393\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602644380\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/801059\"\u003E Local Aboriginal Land Council v Minister Administering the Crown Lands Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (1993) 30 NSWLR 140 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871130392\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/801059/section/139979\"\u003E160\u003C/A\u003E,\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602644380_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E quoting \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871130396\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602644381\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland [No 2]\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (1992) 175 CLR 1 at \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871130395\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140419\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E33.\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602644381_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[562]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()[2]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[562]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/I","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"216\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643831\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_sr_2617\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003ESection \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871129194\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/274763/section/1939\"\u003E36(1)\u003C/A\u003E of the \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871129195\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/274763\"\u003EAboriginal Land Rights Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643831_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E thereby accords with the common law as it existed at the time the \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871129196\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643832\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/274763\"\u003EAboriginal Land Rights Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643832_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E was enacted. As Gageler\u00A0J explained in the \u003CI\u003EBerrima Gaol case\u003C/I\u003E,\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643833\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn277\" name\u003D\"_ftnref277\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[277]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643833_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E at that time: (a)\u00A0common law orthodoxy had caused the Supreme Court of New South Wales to declare in 1847 that \"the waste lands of this Colony are, and ever have been, from the time of its first settlement in 1788, in the Crown\";\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643834\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn278\" name\u003D\"_ftnref278\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[278]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643834_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E (b)\u00A0this common law orthodoxy that the Crown was \"the absolute beneficial owner of all of the land in New South Wales from the time of settlement in 1788\" was not overruled until \u003CI\u003EMabo v Queensland [No 2]\u003C/I\u003E, following which it was accepted that the Crown held radical title over all such land, enabling the Crown to grant estates and interests in the land;\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643835\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn279\" name\u003D\"_ftnref279\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[279]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643835_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E and (c)\u00A0from the enactment of the \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871129197\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643836\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/437713\"\u003ECrown Lands\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643836_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E Alienation Act 1861\u003C/I\u003E (NSW), \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871129198\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643837\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/437713\"\u003ECrown lands\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643837_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E legislation in New South Wales has provided to the effect that \"[a]ny \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871129199\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643838\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/437713\"\u003ECrown Lands\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643838_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E may lawfully be granted in fee simple or dedicated to any public purpose under and subject to the provisions of [that] Act but not otherwise\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643839\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn280\" name\u003D\"_ftnref280\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[280]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_e_t_sr_2617\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643839_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref279\" name\u003D\"_ftn279\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[279]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-7\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871130323\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602644353\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/508815\"\u003E(2016) 260 CLR 232\u003C/A\u003E at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871130324\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/508815/section/194\"\u003E277\u003C/A\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871130325\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/508815/section/194\"\u003E[111]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602644353_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, referring to \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871130328\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602644354\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland [No 2]\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (1992) 175 CLR 1 at \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871130327\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140278\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E54.\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602644354_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","GAGELER CJ.","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[139]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()[1]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[139]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/I[1]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"31\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_sr_1017\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EThe correctness of the conclusion that the \"lands\" vested in the Crown in right of New South Wales to which the definition of \"claimable Crown lands\" in\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643237\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E s\u00A0\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871128688\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/274763/section/1939\"\u003E36(1)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643237_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E refers are confined to physical land is confirmed by the language and, even more so, the substance of the conditions specified in\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643238\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E s \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871128689\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/274763/section/29642\"\u003E36(1)(d)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643238_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E and (e) that those lands \"do not comprise lands\" that are the subject of an application for a determination of native title that has been registered under the \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871128690\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643239\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENative Title Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643239_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E or of an approved determination that native title exists under the \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871128691\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643240\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENative Title Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643240_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E. An application for a determination of native title that has been registered under the \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871128692\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643241\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENative Title Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643241_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E involves an assertion that a person or persons \"hold native title in relation to a specified area of land or waters\"\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643242\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn62\" name\u003D\"_ftnref62\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[62]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643242_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E the registered particulars of which must include \"the area of land or waters covered by the claim\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643243\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn63\" name\u003D\"_ftnref63\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[63]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643243_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E Correspondingly, an approved determination that native title exists under the \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871128693\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643244\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENative Title Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643244_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E involves a determination that \"native title exists in relation to a particular area ... of land or waters\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643245\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn64\" name\u003D\"_ftnref64\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[64]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643245_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E The definition of \"land\" for the purposes of the \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871128694\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643246\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENative Title Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643246_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E is expressed in terms which make clear that it is concerned only with physical land.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643247\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn65\" name\u003D\"_ftnref65\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[65]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643247_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E That is consistent with the definition of \"native title\" for the purposes of the \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871128695\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643248\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENative Title Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643248_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, which relevantly refers to \"the communal, group or individual rights and interests of Aboriginal peoples ... in relation to land or waters\", where \"the rights and interests are possessed under the traditional laws acknowledged, and the traditional customs observed, by the Aboriginal peoples\" and \"the Aboriginal peoples ..., by those laws and customs, have a connection with the land or waters\", and where \"the rights and interests are recognised by the common law of Australia\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643249\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn66\" name\u003D\"_ftnref66\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[66]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643249_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E Indeed, it would be a nonsense for the concept of native title as first recognised by the common law of Australia and as \"recognised, and protected, in accordance with\" the \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871128696\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643250\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENative Title Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn67\" name\u003D\"_ftnref67\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[67]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643250_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E to be sought to be applied to anything other than physical land or waters. Not being \"an institution of the common law\" but having its origin in and being given its content \"by the traditional laws acknowledged by and the traditional customs observed by the [I]ndigenous inhabitants of a territory\",\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643251\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn68\" name\u003D\"_ftnref68\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[68]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602643251_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E native title is a concept that is wholly incapable of meaningful application to a legal or equitable estate or interest in land because a legal or equitable estate or interest in land is the abstract creation of a different system of law.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_e_t_sr_1017\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv title\u003D\"\"\u003E[68]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-9\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_cc_871129524\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/1119338/section/4039\"\u003E539\u003C/A\u003E id\u003D\"bnj_a_1148491_ct_602644076_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[1]/TABLE/TR[20]/TD[2]/P[7]/TEXT()[8]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[1]/TABLE/TR[20]/TD[2]/P[7]/BR[7]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v State of Queensland (No 2) \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E(1992) 175 CLR 1\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1147902_ct_602244839_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E, cited\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[14]/DIV/P/TEXT()[2]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[14]/DIV/P/I","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"29\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1147902_sr_140712\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EGenamson also urges us to interpret compensation legislation liberally in accordance with the general rule of statutory interpretation that clear language must be used in order to impute a legislative intention that valuable property rights are to be appropriated or extinguished without fair compensation.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1147902_ct_602244887\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn13\" name\u003D\"_ftnref13\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[13]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1147902_ct_602244887_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u00A0 Again, that principle does not assist Genamson in this case where no property rights were expropriated or extinguished.\u00A0 In any case, the words used in s 16(1A) are clear.\u00A0 Those words deny “any claim for compensation … whatsoever … for any loss or damage”, subject only to the exceptions of a claim for costs and expenses or actual damage done to the land by the constructing authority.\u00A0 The application of the general rule does not permit this Court to ignore such clear words simply to assuage a sense of unfairness.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1147902_e_t_sr_140712\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-outdent\"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref13\" name\u003D\"_ftn13\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[13]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-9\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003ESee, eg, \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1147902_cc_870472024\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1147902_ct_602244978\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" id\u003D\"bnj_a_1150635_sr_140829\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E[41]\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0The rule that statutory extinguishment of customary property rights can only be achieved through “clear and plain” language is well settled.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1150635_ct_602854276\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn53\" name\u003D\"_ftnref53\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[53]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1150635_ct_602854276_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u00A0 The issue here is whether the wording in s 261(2) of the 1979 Act is sufficiently clear to meet this standard.\u00A0 \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1150635_e_t_sr_140829\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP href\u003D\"https://jade.io/citation/2740298/section/16255\"\u003E691–692\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1150635_ct_602854473_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E.  See also \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1150635_cc_871464649\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1150635_ct_602854474\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/976722\"\u003ENgāti\u00A0Apa\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E, above n 36, at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1150635_cc_871464647\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/976722/section/3504\"\u003E[113]\u003C/A\u003E per Gault P and \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1150635_cc_871464648\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/976722/section/2317\"\u003E[185]\u003C/A\u003E per Tipping J\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1150635_ct_602854474_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E.  \u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","EDELMAN J.","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[116]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()[1]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[116]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/I[1]","Para 69","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"40\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_sr_140890\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EOn this broader view, as Sir\u00A0Anthony Mason rightly said, the suggestion that a decision can be without a ratio decidendi is wrong: \"Every decision has its \u003CI\u003Eratio\u003C/I\u003E.\"\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn53\" name\u003D\"_ftnref53\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[53]\u003C/A\u003E A ratio decidendi exists even if there is no commonality to the essential reasoning of a majority of the Court sufficient to support its orders at anything other than the highest level of generality. That broader view is now, as Nettle\u00A0J has said, \"orthodox\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363146\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn54\" name\u003D\"_ftnref54\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[54]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363146_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E For instance, even if (which is doubtful) there were no commonality at a low level of generality to the essential reasoning of any four members of this Court sufficient to support the orders made in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_cc_866104714\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363147\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo v Queensland [No\u00A02]\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn55\" name\u003D\"_ftnref55\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[55]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363147_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E or in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_cc_866104715\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363148\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/708192\"\u003ELove v The Commonwealth\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E,\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn56\" name\u003D\"_ftnref56\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[56]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363148_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E the ratio decidendi would exist at a higher level of generality by combining the essential reasons of the members of the majority in each of those decisions.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363149\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn57\" name\u003D\"_ftnref57\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[57]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"40\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_sr_140890\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EOn this broader view, as Sir\u00A0Anthony Mason rightly said, the suggestion that a decision can be without a ratio decidendi is wrong: \"Every decision has its \u003CI\u003Eratio\u003C/I\u003E.\"\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn53\" name\u003D\"_ftnref53\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[53]\u003C/A\u003E A ratio decidendi exists even if there is no commonality to the essential reasoning of a majority of the Court sufficient to support its orders at anything other than the highest level of generality. That broader view is now, as Nettle\u00A0J has said, \"orthodox\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363146\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn54\" name\u003D\"_ftnref54\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[54]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363146_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E For instance, even if (which is doubtful) there were no commonality at a low level of generality to the essential reasoning of any four members of this Court sufficient to support the orders made in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_cc_866104714\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363147\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo v Queensland [No\u00A02]\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn55\" name\u003D\"_ftnref55\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[55]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363147_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E or in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_cc_866104715\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363148\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/708192\"\u003ELove v The Commonwealth\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E,\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn56\" name\u003D\"_ftnref56\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[56]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363148_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E the ratio decidendi would exist at a higher level of generality by combining the essential reasons of the members of the majority in each of those decisions.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363149\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn57\" name\u003D\"_ftnref57\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[57]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"40\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_sr_140890\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EOn this broader view, as Sir\u00A0Anthony Mason rightly said, the suggestion that a decision can be without a ratio decidendi is wrong: \"Every decision has its \u003CI\u003Eratio\u003C/I\u003E.\"\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn53\" name\u003D\"_ftnref53\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[53]\u003C/A\u003E A ratio decidendi exists even if there is no commonality to the essential reasoning of a majority of the Court sufficient to support its orders at anything other than the highest level of generality. That broader view is now, as Nettle\u00A0J has said, \"orthodox\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363146\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn54\" name\u003D\"_ftnref54\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[54]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363146_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E For instance, even if (which is doubtful) there were no commonality at a low level of generality to the essential reasoning of any four members of this Court sufficient to support the orders made in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_cc_866104714\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363147\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland [No\u00A02]\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn55\" name\u003D\"_ftnref55\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E[55]\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363147_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E or in \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_cc_866104715\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363148\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/708192\"\u003ELove v The Commonwealth\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E,\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn56\" name\u003D\"_ftnref56\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[56]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363148_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E the ratio decidendi would exist at a higher level of generality by combining the essential reasons of the members of the majority in each of those decisions.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363149\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn57\" name\u003D\"_ftnref57\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[57]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_e_t_sr_140890\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145922_ct_599363149_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","Mabo and Others v Queensland (No. 2)","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL/LI[35]/P/TEXT()[2]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL/LI[35]/P/I","Para 35","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"35\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_sr_140690\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EThe Defendant’s argument cannot be sustained, on the basis of legal authority upon which I am bound to follow.\u00A0 In so far as the Defendant’s argument challenges the acquisition of sovereignty by the Crown, and therefore its ability to grant title, as stated in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_cc_864521541\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234398\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo and Others v Queensland (No. 2) \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234398_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E(footnotes omitted):\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234399\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn32\" name\u003D\"_ftnref32\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[32]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234399_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CB\u003EThe acquisition of sovereignty \u003C/B\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CP\u003E\"The acquisition of territory by a sovereign state for the first time is an act of state which cannot be challenged, controlled or interfered with by the courts of that state.\" \u003C/P\u003E\u003CP\u003EThis principle, stated by Gibbs J. in the Seas and Submerged Lands Case, precludes any contest between the executive and the judicial branches of government as to whether a territory is or is not within the Crown\u0027s Dominions. The Murray Islands were annexed by an exercise of the prerogative evidenced by the Letters Patent; a mode of acquisition recognized by the common law as a valid means of acquiring sovereignty over foreign territory. The recognition is accorded simply on the footing that such a prerogative act is an act of State the validity of which is not justiciable in the municipal courts.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_e_t_sr_140690\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL/LI[37]/P/I/TEXT()","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL/LI[37]/P/I","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"37\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_sr_140276\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EFurther to that argument, that the Crown’s basis to grant title to land is otherwise questionable or that there is some alternate or other system of land ownership, must be rejected.\u00A0 Again, it was stated in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_cc_864521542\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234401\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo No.\u00A02\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234401_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E:\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234402\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn36\" name\u003D\"_ftnref36\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[36]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234402_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP\u003EIt is not surprising that the fiction that land granted by the Crown had been beneficially owned by the Crown was translated to the colonies and that Crown grants should be seen as the foundation of the doctrine of tenure which is an essential principle of our land law. It is far too late in the day to contemplate an allodial or other system of land ownership. Land in Australia which has been granted by the Crown is held on a tenure of some kind and the titles acquired under the accepted land law cannot be disturbed….\u003C/P\u003E\u003CP\u003EThe radical title is a postulate of the doctrine of tenure and a concomitant of sovereignty. As a sovereign enjoys supreme legal authority in and over a territory, the sovereign has power to prescribe what parcels of land and what interests in those parcels should be enjoyed by others and what parcels of land should be kept as the sovereign\u0027s beneficial demesne.\u003C/P\u003E\u003CP\u003EBy attributing to the Crown a radical title to all land within a territory over which the Crown has assumed sovereignty, the common law enabled the Crown, in exercise of its sovereign power, to grant an interest in land to be held of the Crown or to acquire land for the Crown\u0027s demesne. \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_e_t_sr_140276\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[46]/DIV/P/TEXT()[2]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[46]/DIV/P/I[1]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"35\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_sr_140690\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EThe Defendant’s argument cannot be sustained, on the basis of legal authority upon which I am bound to follow.\u00A0 In so far as the Defendant’s argument challenges the acquisition of sovereignty by the Crown, and therefore its ability to grant title, as stated in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_cc_864521541\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234398\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo and Others v Queensland (No. 2) \u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234398_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E(footnotes omitted):\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234399\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn32\" name\u003D\"_ftnref32\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[32]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234399_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CB\u003EThe acquisition of sovereignty \u003C/B\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CP\u003E\"The acquisition of territory by a sovereign state for the first time is an act of state which cannot be challenged, controlled or interfered with by the courts of that state.\" \u003C/P\u003E\u003CP\u003EThis principle, stated by Gibbs J. in the Seas and Submerged Lands Case, precludes any contest between the executive and the judicial branches of government as to whether a territory is or is not within the Crown\u0027s Dominions. The Murray Islands were annexed by an exercise of the prerogative evidenced by the Letters Patent; a mode of acquisition recognized by the common law as a valid means of acquiring sovereignty over foreign territory. The recognition is accorded simply on the footing that such a prerogative act is an act of State the validity of which is not justiciable in the municipal courts.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_e_t_sr_140690\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\" style\u003D\"font-size:80%; \"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref32\" name\u003D\"_ftn32\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[32]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CB\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-9\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/B\u003E\u00A0\u00A0 \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_cc_864521614\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234445\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo and Others v Queensland (No. 2)\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (1992) 175 CLR 1 at \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_cc_864521613\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140349\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E31\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E per Brennan J\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234445_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (“\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003EMabo No. 2”\u003C/I\u003E).\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[50]/DIV/P/TEXT()[2]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[50]/DIV/P/I","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"37\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_sr_140276\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EFurther to that argument, that the Crown’s basis to grant title to land is otherwise questionable or that there is some alternate or other system of land ownership, must be rejected.\u00A0 Again, it was stated in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_cc_864521542\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234401\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo No.\u00A02\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234401_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E:\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234402\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn36\" name\u003D\"_ftnref36\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[36]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234402_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP\u003EIt is not surprising that the fiction that land granted by the Crown had been beneficially owned by the Crown was translated to the colonies and that Crown grants should be seen as the foundation of the doctrine of tenure which is an essential principle of our land law. It is far too late in the day to contemplate an allodial or other system of land ownership. Land in Australia which has been granted by the Crown is held on a tenure of some kind and the titles acquired under the accepted land law cannot be disturbed….\u003C/P\u003E\u003CP\u003EThe radical title is a postulate of the doctrine of tenure and a concomitant of sovereignty. As a sovereign enjoys supreme legal authority in and over a territory, the sovereign has power to prescribe what parcels of land and what interests in those parcels should be enjoyed by others and what parcels of land should be kept as the sovereign\u0027s beneficial demesne.\u003C/P\u003E\u003CP\u003EBy attributing to the Crown a radical title to all land within a territory over which the Crown has assumed sovereignty, the common law enabled the Crown, in exercise of its sovereign power, to grant an interest in land to be held of the Crown or to acquire land for the Crown\u0027s demesne. \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_e_t_sr_140276\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\" style\u003D\"font-size:80%; \"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref36\" name\u003D\"_ftn36\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[36]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CB\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-9\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/B\u003E\u00A0\u00A0 \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_cc_864521617\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234447\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo No. 2\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E at pp \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_cc_864521616\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/139929\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E47-48\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E per Brennan J\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234447_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_cc_864521509\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234358\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" Morgan\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (1861) 43 ER 1056\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234359_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E;\u003CI\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_cc_864521513\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234360\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/258317\"\u003EWestpac Banking Corporation v Mason\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [2011] NSWSC 1241\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1145111_ct_598234360_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, referred to.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[1]/TABLE[1]/TR[11]/TD[2]/BLOCKQUOTE[82]/P/TEXT()","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[1]/TABLE[1]/TR[11]/TD[2]/BLOCKQUOTE[82]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1143246_cc_875141494\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1143246_ct_605471550\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland (No 2)\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (1992) 175 CLR 1\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1143246_ct_605471550_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL/LI[871]/P/TEXT()[2]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL/LI[871]/P/I","Para 871","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"871\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1143246_sr_61451\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EFirst, the applicants relied on various historical statements made by colonial officials and others about the nature of the relationship between colonies and the indigenous populations of those colonies.\u00A0 Those statements suggested that colonial administrators or governments had a duty to protect the “natives”.\u00A0 The applicants also relied on some colonial enactments that manifested an intention on the part of colonial governments to protect aboriginal people.\u00A0 The statements and enactments relied on by the applicants were, however, made before the Commonwealth came into being and did not directly relate to the relationship between the Commonwealth and Torres Strait Islanders.\u00A0 The applicants also rightly conceded that those statements and enactments, considered in the modern context, could now properly be viewed as paternalistic.\u00A0 In the same vein, the applicants relied on the fact that one of the purposes for which the Torres Strait was annexed by the Colony of Queensland in 1879 was said to be the “protection of the native inhabitants of the islands”: see \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1143246_cc_875141945\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1143246_ct_605471784\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland (No 2)\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (1992) 175 CLR 1 at \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1143246_cc_875141944\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/139992\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E23\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1143246_ct_605471784_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CB\u003E\u003CI\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/I\u003E\u003C/B\u003E). \u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1143246_e_t_sr_61451\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/DOC/BLOCK[422]/OL/LI[2]/BLOCK/BLOCK/BLOCK/P/TEXT()[2]","/DOC/BLOCK[422]/OL/LI[2]/BLOCK/BLOCK/BLOCK/P/INLINE","/DOC/BLOCK[422]/BLOCK[2]/BLOCK[2]/BLOCK[2]/P/TEXT()[2]","/DOC/BLOCK[422]/BLOCK[2]/BLOCK[2]/BLOCK[2]/P/INLINE","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003Cp\u003EMembership of the Indigenous people depends on biological descent from the Indigenous people and on mutual recognition of a particular person’s membership by that person and by the elders or other persons enjoying traditional authority among those people.\u003Cinline class\u003D\"footnote\" number\u003D\"1002\"\u003E\u003Cinline class\u003D\"footnote-number\"\u003E\u003Ca id\u003D\"bnj_a_1049904_cc_874502245\"\u003E\u003C/a\u003E\u003Ca id\u003D\"bnj_a_1049904_ct_605043720\"\u003E\u003C/a\u003E\u003Ca class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E1002\u003C/a\u003E\u003Ca id\u003D\"bnj_a_1049904_ct_605043720_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/a\u003E\u003C/inline\u003E\u003C/inline\u003E \u003C/p\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003Cp\u003E \u003Cinline class\u003D\"italic\"\u003E\u003Ca id\u003D\"bnj_a_1049904_cc_874502242\"\u003E\u003C/a\u003E\u003Ca id\u003D\"bnj_a_1049904_ct_605043719\"\u003E\u003C/a\u003E\u003Ca class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003Cspan class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland (No 2)\u003C/span\u003E\u003C/a\u003E\u003C/inline\u003E\u003Cspan class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (1992) 175 CLR 1, \u003C/span\u003E\u003Ca id\u003D\"bnj_a_1049904_cc_874502244\"\u003E\u003C/a\u003E\u003Ca class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140001\"\u003E\u003Cspan class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E70\u003C/span\u003E\u003C/a\u003E\u003Cspan class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (Brennan J)\u003C/span\u003E\u003Ca id\u003D\"bnj_a_1049904_ct_605043719_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/a\u003E\u003Cspan class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E.\u003C/span\u003E\u003C/p\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003Cp\u003EMembership of the Indigenous people depends on biological descent from the Indigenous people and on mutual recognition of a particular person’s membership by that person and by the elders or other persons enjoying traditional authority among those people.\u003Cinline class\u003D\"footnote\" number\u003D\"1002\"\u003E\u003Cinline class\u003D\"footnote-number\"\u003E\u003Ca id\u003D\"bnj_a_1049904_cc_874502245\"\u003E\u003C/a\u003E\u003Ca id\u003D\"bnj_a_1049904_ct_605043720\"\u003E\u003C/a\u003E\u003Ca class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003Cspan class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E1002\u003C/span\u003E\u003C/a\u003E\u003Ca id\u003D\"bnj_a_1049904_ct_605043720_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/a\u003E\u003C/inline\u003E\u003C/inline\u003E\u003Cspan class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E \u003C/span\u003E\u003C/p\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","BEECH-JONES J.","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[1214]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()[2]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[1214]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/I[2]","Para 477","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"451\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1136772_sr_14080\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EThese provisions enable the administrator to manage the affairs and property of the C\u00A0\u0026amp;\u00A0G Division by furthering the CFMEU\u0027s objects as defined in the CFMEU Rules, including the political objects described above, such as by making political donations. However, they are not merely facultative. The conferral of broad powers to manage the affairs and property of the C\u00A0\u0026amp;\u00A0G Division the exercise of which is subject to the duties in s\u00A0323K may warrant the characterisation of the administrator as a fiduciary vis\u2011a\u2011vis the members of the C\u00A0\u0026amp;\u00A0G Division.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1136772_ct_595095600\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn603\" name\u003D\"_ftnref603\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[603]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1136772_ct_595095600_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E As such the administrator could not fetter his or her discretionary powers\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn604\" name\u003D\"_ftnref604\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[604]\u003C/A\u003E and would be under a duty to consider their exercise,\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1136772_ct_595095601\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn605\" name\u003D\"_ftnref605\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[605]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1136772_ct_595095601_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E doing so honestly and in good faith \"upon [a] genuine consideration\" of relevant matters by taking an informed view of whether or not they should be exercised.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1136772_ct_595095602\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn606\" name\u003D\"_ftnref606\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[606]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1136772_ct_595095602_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E Absent the characterisation of the administrator as a fiduciary, the conferral of discretionary functions and powers on the administrator by statute carries with it at least an implied obligation to consider their exercise\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1136772_ct_595095603\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn607\" name\u003D\"_ftnref607\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[607]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1136772_ct_595095603_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E and they likewise could not be unduly fettered.\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn608\" name\u003D\"_ftnref608\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[608]\u003C/A\u003E In both respects, the administrator is subject to the supervision of the courts in the exercise of those functions and powers.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1136772_e_t_sr_14080\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref605\" name\u003D\"_ftn605\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[605]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-7\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1136772_cc_860063852\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1136772_ct_595096454\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-citable\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/citation/7779473\"\u003EIn re Gulbenkian\u0027s Settlements \u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E[1970] AC 508 at \u003CA Trusts in Australia\u003C/I\u003E, 8th ed (2016) at 326 [16-07].\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","Secretary, Department of Health and Community Services v JWB and SMB (“Marion\u0027s Case”)","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL/LI[48]/P/TEXT()[3]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL/LI[48]/P/I[2]","Para 48","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"48\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1135159_sr_140756\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EThe legal principles pronounced in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1135159_cc_852226521\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1135159_ct_590760514\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-citable\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/citation/2809193\"\u003EGillick v West Norfolk and Wisbech Area Health Authority\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [1986] AC 112\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1135159_ct_590760514_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, which were confirmed by the High Court in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1135159_cc_852226523\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1135159_ct_590760515\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003ESecretary, Department of Health and Community Services v JWB and SMB (“Marion’s Case”) \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1135159_cc_852226522\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140775\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E(1992) 175 CLR 189\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1135159_ct_590760515_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E, are as follows: \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP style\u003D\"font-size:94%; \"\u003EA child is capable of giving informed consent when he or she achieves a sufficient understanding and intelligence to enable him or her to understand fully what treatment is proposed.\u00A0 \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1135159_e_t_sr_140756\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1135159_cc_852226512\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1135159_ct_590760507\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003ESecretary, Department of Health and Community Services v JWB and SMB (“Marion’s Case”) \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1135159_cc_852226511\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140775\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E(1992) 175 CLR 189\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1135159_ct_590760507_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL/LI[36]/P/TEXT()[2]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL/LI[36]/P/I[1]","Para 36","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"36\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_sr_1155\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EThe \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_cc_848663365\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_ct_588758189\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENTA\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_ct_588758189_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E commences with a Preamble that “sets out considerations taken into account by the Parliament in enacting the law that follows”.  Among other things, the Preamble recognises that “the people whose descendants are now known as Aboriginal peoples and Torres Strait Islanders were the inhabitants of Australia before European settlement”, and the consequences of their progressive dispossession of their lands.  As is acknowledged in the Preamble, the \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_cc_848663366\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_ct_588758190\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENTA\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_ct_588758190_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E was enacted in response to the High Court’s decision in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_cc_848663368\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_ct_588758191\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland (No\u00A02)\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (1992) 175 CLR 1\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_ct_588758191_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E, as a special law for peoples of the aboriginal race within\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_ct_588758192\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E s \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_cc_848663369\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/260323/section/20751\"\u003E51(xxvi)\u003C/A\u003E of the \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_cc_848663370\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/260323\"\u003EConstitution\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_ct_588758192_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E.  The Preamble declares the intention of the people of Australia “to rectify the consequences of past injustices by the special measures contained in this Act … for securing the adequate advancement and protection of Aboriginal peoples and Torres Strait Islanders” and “to ensure that Aboriginal peoples and Torres Strait Islanders receive the full recognition and status within the Australian nation to which history, their prior rights and interests, and their rich and diverse culture, fully entitle them to aspire”.  The Preamble also states that the \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_cc_848663371\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_ct_588758193\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENTA\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_ct_588758193_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E is intended, for the purposes of Art 1(4) of the \u003CI\u003EInternational Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Racial Discrimination\u003C/I\u003E (opened for signature 21 December 1965, 660 UNTS 196 (entered into force 4 January 1969)) (\u003CB\u003EICERD\u003C/B\u003E) and the \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_cc_848663372\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_ct_588758194\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/219758\"\u003ERacial Discrimination Act 1975\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/219758\"\u003E (Cth)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_ct_588758194_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E (\u003CB\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_cc_848663373\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_ct_588758195\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/219758\"\u003ERDA\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_ct_588758195_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/B\u003E), to be a special measure for the advancement and protection of Aboriginal peoples and Torres Strait Islanders.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_e_t_sr_1155\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[1]/TABLE[1]/TR[11]/TD[2]/P[39]/TEXT()","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[1]/TABLE[1]/TR[11]/TD[2]/P[39]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_cc_848663160\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_ct_588758058\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland (No\u00A02)\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (1992) 175 CLR 1\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1132392_ct_588758058_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[2099]/DIV/P/TEXT()[3]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[2099]/DIV/P/I[2]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"2324\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_sr_62863\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EThe applicant submits that the proper approach to the exercise of the Commonwealth’s statutory functions under the \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_cc_865087428\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_ct_598591372\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218346\"\u003EMigration Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_ct_598591372_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E is informed by Australia’s international obligations.  The applicant submits this is recognised in the IDS.  He contends that the Australian community has never accepted standards in breach of internationally accepted human rights.  The applicant relies on Articles 7 and 10 of the \u003CI\u003EInternational Covenant on Civil and Political Rights.\u003C/I\u003E\u00A0 Article 7 provides that no-one shall be subject to torture or cruel, inhumane or degrading treatment or punishment. Article 10 requires state parties to treat all persons deprived of their liberty with humanity and with respect for the inherent dignity of the human person.\u00A0 These obligations have not been imported into Australian domestic law.\u00A0 Since the High Court’s judgment in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_cc_865087429\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_ct_598591373\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/66984\"\u003EKoowarta v Bjelke Petersen\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn2066\" name\u003D\"_ftnref2066\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[2066]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_ct_598591373_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E it has been well settled that the ratification by the executive of an international treaty does not give rise to a cause of action in domestic law until it is incorporated by legislation. However, the High Court has recognised that the adoption of international treaties and covenants by Australia can influence the development of the common law.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_ct_598591374\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn2067\" name\u003D\"_ftnref2067\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[2067]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_e_t_sr_62863\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_ct_598591374_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins\"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref2067\" name\u003D\"_ftn2067\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[2067]\u003C/A\u003E \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_cc_865088373\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_ct_598591912\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67720\"\u003EDietrich v The Queen \u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E(1992) 177 CLR 292 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_cc_865088372\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67720/section/139943\"\u003E321\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_ct_598591912_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_cc_865088376\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_ct_598591913\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland (No. 2) \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E(1992) 175 CLR 1 at \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_cc_865088375\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140174\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E42.\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1133230_ct_598591913_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[3]/BLOCKQUOTE/P[9]/TEXT()[35]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[3]/BLOCKQUOTE/P[9]/I[35]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html;
