1\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856279_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E  \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL[1]/LI[36]/P/TEXT()[2]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"36\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_sr_1155\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EThe emphasis on traditional laws and customs in\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856321\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E s \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_cc_862614350\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549/section/9183\"\u003E223(1)(a)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856321_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E and\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856322\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E s \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_cc_862614351\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549/section/9119\"\u003E223(1)(b)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856322_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E reflects the reasoning of the High Court in \u003CI\u003EMabo v Queensland (No 2\u003C/I\u003E) \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_cc_862614352\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856323\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E(1992) 175 CLR 1\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856323_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CB\u003E\u003CI\u003EMabo\u00A0(No\u00A02)\u003C/I\u003E\u003C/B\u003E):\u00A0\u00A0the rights and interests recognised by the common law are those pre-existing rights and interests held at the time that the British Crown asserted sovereignty over the colonies and that survived both the assertion of sovereignty and the acquisition of radical title over the annexed land and waters that came with it.\u00A0 It is largely for that reason that the focus in applications of the present kind is initially upon the rights and interests held by the ancestors of the present day claimants, especially (but not exclusively) in cases where the rights and interests are said to be transmissible by descent under Aboriginal traditional laws and customs.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_e_t_sr_1155\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL[1]/LI[38]/BLOCKQUOTE/BLOCKQUOTE/P[1]/TEXT()[5]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL[1]/LI[38]/BLOCKQUOTE/BLOCKQUOTE/P[1]/I[4]","Para 38","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"38\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_sr_1252\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EThe Full Court (Finn, Sundberg and Mansfield\u00A0JJ) in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_cc_862614357\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856326\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/69372\"\u003EBodney v Bennell\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (2008) 167 FCR 84\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856326_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E further explained the concept of a “society” and the related concept of “connection”, as that word is used in\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856327\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E s \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_cc_862614358\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549/section/9119\"\u003E223(1)(b)\u003C/A\u003E of the \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_cc_862614359\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENT Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856327_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E.  It is preferable to extract that summary in full as a correct statement of the law:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\" style\u003D\"font-size:94%; \"\u003E46\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-10\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_cc_862614361\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856328\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68370\"\u003EMembers of the Yorta Yorta Aboriginal Community v Victoria\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (2002) 214 CLR 422\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856328_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E (\u003CI\u003EYorta Yorta HC\u003C/I\u003E) draws attention to three separate but related concepts:\u00A0\u00A0society; laws and customs; and rights and interests.\u00A0 The first is not found in the Act, but is referred to at length in \u003CI\u003EYorta Yorta HC\u003C/I\u003E.\u00A0 The second and third are related in the manner first explained by Brennan\u00A0J in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_cc_862614364\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856329\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland (No 2)\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (1992) 175 CLR 1 at \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_cc_862614363\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/141020\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E58\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856329_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003EMabo (No 2)\u003C/I\u003E).\u00A0 In \u003CI\u003EYorta Yorta HC\u003C/I\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_cc_862614365\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856330\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68370\"\u003E214 CLR 422\u003C/A\u003E the majority said at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_cc_862614366\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68370/section/1625\"\u003E[49]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856330_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E that ‘law and custom arise out of and, in important respects, go to define a particular society’, and that ‘‘society’ is to be understood as a body of persons united in and by its acknowledgment and observance of a body of law and customs’.  At [50] the majority said that ‘to speak of rights and interests possessed under an identified body of laws and customs is, therefore, to speak of rights and interests that are the creatures of the laws and customs of a particular society that exists as a group which acknowledges and observes those laws and customs’.  Their Honours spoke of the traditional laws and customs as constituting a normative system which possesses normative rules which give rise to rights and interests in relation to land and water.\u003C/P\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\" style\u003D\"font-size:94%; \"\u003E47\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-10\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003EBecause it is the normative system that is the source of the rights and interests, it is necessary in order to prove native title that the normative system has had a continuous existence and vitality since sovereignty.\u00A0 If that normative system has not existed throughout that period, the rights and interests which owe their existence to that system will have ceased to exist:\u00A0\u00A0\u003CI\u003EYorta Yorta HC\u003C/I\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_cc_862614367\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856331\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68370\"\u003E214 CLR 422\u003C/A\u003E at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_cc_862614368\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68370/section/777\"\u003E[47]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_ct_596856331_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E.  It is therefore necessary for native title claimants to show that the normative system that existed at sovereignty is substantially the same as the one that exists today.  If it is not, then any rights and interests are not ‘possessed under the traditional laws acknowledged and traditional customs observed’.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131156_e_t_sr_1252\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL[13]/LI[3]/P/TEXT()[2]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL[13]/LI[3]/P/I","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"32\"\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131235_sr_260\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CP\u003EIn his oral submissions the Defendant spoke about “fraud” and similar issues at some length with a restrained and respectful, but nonetheless sincere, passion. With a quietly-spoken and steady dignity he addressed in general terms the circumstances in which the Australian continent was settled by Europeans in the 18th century and afterwards, and how the Indigenous people were dispossessed from the land. He spoke about the Nyangbul people, the Nyangbul Jagan, and his allegiance to what he sometimes described as a “higher law” or a “higher authority”, amongst other things. He referred to a want of self-determination variously for himself, the Nyangbul people and others, the want of a treaty or other agreement and the fiction of terra nullius which was rejected by the High Court in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131235_cc_842254147\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131235_ct_585602196\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland (No 2)\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (1995) 175 CLR 1; [1992] HCA 23\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131235_ct_585602196_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E. \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/P\u003E \u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131235_e_t_sr_260\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","Mabo v Queensland (No 2).","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL[42]/LI[1]/P/TEXT()[3]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL[42]/LI[1]/P/I[2]","Para 140","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL start\u003D\"140\"\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131235_e_t_sr_665\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"140\"\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131235_sr_2460\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CP\u003EIn \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131235_cc_842254293\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131235_ct_585602284\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/708192\"\u003ELove v The Commonwealth\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131235_ct_585602284_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E, the High Court had to consider the construction of the word \"aliens\" in the context of s 51 (xix), and having regard to the position of \"Aboriginal Australians\" according to the tripartite test in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131235_cc_842254294\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131235_ct_585602285\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland (No\u00A02). \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131235_ct_585602285_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EThere is nothing in \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131235_cc_842254295\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131235_ct_585602286\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/708192\"\u003ELove v The Commonwealth \u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131235_ct_585602286_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003Ewhich is relevant to the claims made by the Plaintiff in these proceedings.\u003C/P\u003E \u003C/LI\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1131235_e_t_sr_2460\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[1]/TABLE[1]/TR[11]/TD[2]/P[12]/TEXT()","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[1]/TABLE[1]/TR[11]/TD[2]/P[12]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128916_cc_839836038\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128916_ct_584132682\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland (No 2) \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E(1992) 175 CLR 1\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128916_ct_584132682_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL[1]/LI[100]/P/TEXT()[3]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL[1]/LI[100]/P/I[2]","Para 100","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"100\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128916_sr_1179\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EThe plurality in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128916_cc_839836207\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128916_ct_584132780\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68370\"\u003EYorta Yorta \u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003Ewas careful to note that reference to a normative system of traditional laws and customs may be distracting if undue attention is given to the word ‘system’, particularly if it were to be understood as confined in its application to systems of law that have the characteristics of a European body of written laws (at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128916_cc_839836206\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68370/section/139989\"\u003E[39]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128916_ct_584132780_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E). Nonetheless, the plurality observed that the term recognises the fundamental premise from which the decision in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128916_cc_839836210\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128916_ct_584132781\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland (No 2) \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E(1992) 175 CLR 1 proceeded, that the laws and customs of the indigenous peoples of Australia constituted bodies of normative rules which could give rise to, and had in fact given rise to, rights and interests in relation to land or waters (at \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128916_cc_839836209\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140890\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E[40]\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128916_ct_584132781_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E). Accordingly, the laws and customs in which the relevant rights or interests are founded must be laws or customs having a normative content and deriving from a body of norms or normative system that existed before sovereignty (at [38] and [42]). In \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128916_cc_839836212\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128916_ct_584132782\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/307219\"\u003EWyman No 2\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E, Jagot J observed (at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128916_cc_839836211\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/307219/section/24069\"\u003E[455]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128916_ct_584132782_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E) that “normative content” means established behavioural norms in accordance with the recognised and acknowledged demands for conformity of a society. \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128916_e_t_sr_1179\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[23]/DIV/P/TEXT()[5]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[23]/DIV/P/I[4]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"10\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_sr_140725\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EThe short answers to the arguments are, firstly, that all Aboriginal people are subject to the laws of the Commonwealth and the laws of the States and Territories in which they live. There is a wealth of authority confirming that proposition from both this Court\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_ct_583954812\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn4\" name\u003D\"_ftnref4\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[4]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_ct_583954812_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E and the High Court,\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_ct_583954813\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn5\" name\u003D\"_ftnref5\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[5]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_ct_583954813_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E as well as superior courts in other States.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_ct_583954814\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn6\" name\u003D\"_ftnref6\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[6]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_ct_583954814_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E None of the cases referred to by Oldroyd have decided any differently.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_ct_583954815\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn7\" name\u003D\"_ftnref7\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[7]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_ct_583954815_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_e_t_sr_140725\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\" style\u003D\"font-size:80%; \"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref5\" name\u003D\"_ftn5\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[5]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CB\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-10\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/B\u003EFor example, \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_cc_839504062\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_ct_583954851\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/66566\"\u003ENew South Wales v Commonwealth\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (1975) 135 CLR 337 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_cc_839504061\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/66566/section/140766\"\u003E388\u003C/A\u003E per Gibbs J\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_ct_583954851_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_cc_839504066\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_ct_583954852\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/188346\"\u003ECoe v Commonwealth\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (1979) 53 ALJR 403 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_cc_839504064\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/188346/section/140102\"\u003E408\u003C/A\u003E per Gibbs J (Aickin J agreeing), at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_cc_839504065\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/188346/section/140153\"\u003E410\u003C/A\u003E per Jacobs J\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_ct_583954852_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E (Murphy J agreeing); \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_cc_839504069\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_ct_583954853\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67773\"\u003ECoe v Commonwealth\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (1993) 68 ALJR 110 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_cc_839504068\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67773/section/2656\"\u003E114-116\u003C/A\u003E per Mason CJ\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_ct_583954853_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, referring to \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_cc_839504071\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_ct_583954854\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland (No 2)\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (1992) 175 CLR 1\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1128689_ct_583954854_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E. In \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003EMabo v Queensland (No 2)\u003C/I\u003E, the High Court accepted that, upon European settlement of the Australian colonies, the English settlers brought with them the law of England and the common law thus became the common law of all subjects within the colony, including Aboriginal people (see 38 per Brennan J). \u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[610]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()","Para 191","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"160\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_sr_140764\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EBy this, his Honour meant several things. First, that the British acquisition of sovereignty over the territories of Australia in 1788 established political control over those territories but sovereignty did not carry with it absolute or \"beneficial\" ownership of land within those territories. Instead, on sovereignty, the Crown acquired a radical title to those lands\u00A0—\u00A0a title that co-exists with the rights and interests of the indigenous inhabitants as they existed at sovereignty and insofar as the common law could recognise those rights and interests. Second, that this radical title gave the Crown \"supreme legal authority in and over a territory ... to prescribe what parcels of land and what interests in those parcels should be enjoyed by others and what parcels of land should be kept as the sovereign\u0027s beneficial demesne\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184372\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn303\" name\u003D\"_ftnref303\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[303]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184372_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E Third, that the exercise of this supreme legal authority of the Crown in respect of land within its Australian territories did not occur instantaneously in 1788 by the establishment of one colony at Sydney and associated declarations, but occurred over time in response to British (and European) occupation of greater and greater areas of land and as a result of which the indigenous inhabitants were progressively displaced from land over which they had previously exercised dominion in accordance with their own laws and customs.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184373\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn304\" name\u003D\"_ftnref304\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[304]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_140764\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_61314_sr.t_From absolute ownership to radical title_sr.x_24\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184373_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref304\" name\u003D\"_ftn304\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[304]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-7\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186368\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184766\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E(1992) 175 CLR 1\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E at \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186369\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/4323\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E68-69\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA JJ.","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL/LI[18]/P[2]/TEXT()[3]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL/LI[18]/P[2]/I[1]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[40]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()[1]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"18\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184001\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_sr_1035\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003ESection \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185508\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549/section/8612\"\u003E223(1)\u003C/A\u003E of the \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185509\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENative Title Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184001_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003C/I\u003Edefines the expression \"native title\" or \"native title rights and interests\"\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184002\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn18\" name\u003D\"_ftnref18\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[18]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184002_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E relevantly as follows: \u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\"\u003CI\u003ECommon law rights and interests\u003C/I\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E(1) \u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-10\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003EThe expression \u003CB\u003E\u003CI\u003Enative title\u003C/I\u003E\u003C/B\u003E or \u003CB\u003E\u003CI\u003Enative title rights and interests\u003C/I\u003E\u003C/B\u003E means the communal, group or individual rights and interests of Aboriginal peoples or Torres Strait Islanders in relation to land or waters, where:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E(a)\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-10\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003Ethe rights and interests are possessed under the traditional laws acknowledged, and the traditional customs observed, by\u00A0the Aboriginal peoples or Torres Strait Islanders; and \u003C/P\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E(b) \u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-10\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003Ethe Aboriginal peoples or Torres Strait Islanders, \u003CI\u003Eby those laws and customs\u003C/I\u003E, \u003CI\u003Ehave a connection with the land or waters\u003C/I\u003E; and\u003C/P\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E(c) \u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-10\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003Ethe rights and interests are recognised by the common law of Australia.\" (emphasis added)\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CP\u003EThe immediately relevant elements in the definition in\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184003\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E s \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185510\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549/section/8612\"\u003E223(1)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184003_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E of \"native title\" and \"native title rights and interests\" are based on this Court\u0027s decision in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185511\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184004\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo v Queensland [No 2]\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E,\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn19\" name\u003D\"_ftnref19\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[19]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184004_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E and have remained constant since the \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185512\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184005\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENative Title Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184005_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003C/I\u003Ewas passed.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184006\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn20\" name\u003D\"_ftnref20\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[20]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184006_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_1035\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref19\" name\u003D\"_ftn19\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[19]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-9\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185682\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184469\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E(1992) 175 CLR 1\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184469_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E. See also \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185686\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184470\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/69372\"\u003EBodney v Bennell \u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E(2008) 167 FCR 84 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185684\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/69372/section/57\"\u003E126\u003C/A\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185685\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/69372/section/57\"\u003E[163]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184470_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E.\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[608]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"160\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_sr_140764\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EBy this, his Honour meant several things. First, that the British acquisition of sovereignty over the territories of Australia in 1788 established political control over those territories but sovereignty did not carry with it absolute or \"beneficial\" ownership of land within those territories. Instead, on sovereignty, the Crown acquired a radical title to those lands\u00A0—\u00A0a title that co-exists with the rights and interests of the indigenous inhabitants as they existed at sovereignty and insofar as the common law could recognise those rights and interests. Second, that this radical title gave the Crown \"supreme legal authority in and over a territory ... to prescribe what parcels of land and what interests in those parcels should be enjoyed by others and what parcels of land should be kept as the sovereign\u0027s beneficial demesne\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184372\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn303\" name\u003D\"_ftnref303\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[303]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184372_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E Third, that the exercise of this supreme legal authority of the Crown in respect of land within its Australian territories did not occur instantaneously in 1788 by the establishment of one colony at Sydney and associated declarations, but occurred over time in response to British (and European) occupation of greater and greater areas of land and as a result of which the indigenous inhabitants were progressively displaced from land over which they had previously exercised dominion in accordance with their own laws and customs.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184373\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn304\" name\u003D\"_ftnref304\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[304]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_140764\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_61314_sr.t_From absolute ownership to radical title_sr.x_24\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184373_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref303\" name\u003D\"_ftn303\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[303]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-7\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186366\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184765\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E(1992) 175 CLR 1\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E at \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186367\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140703\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E48.\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184765_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[600]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"159\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_sr_3102\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EThe recognition of native title rights and interests at common law resulting from this Court\u0027s decision in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185620\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184363\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo v Queensland [No\u00A02]\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn298\" name\u003D\"_ftnref298\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[298]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184363_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E and subsequently under the \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185621\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184364\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENative Title Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184364_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E is rooted in the specific history of the British colonisation and settlement of Australia. As social, economic, political, and demographic pressures evolved in Great Britain over the 18th and 19th centuries, British people (and people from other parts of Europe), either unwillingly (in the case of British convicts) or willingly, made their way to \"the colonies\" including Australia in increasing numbers. As Brennan J observed in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184365\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184365_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E, \"English colonists were, in the eye of the common law, entitled to live under the common law of England which Blackstone described as their \u0027birthright\u0027\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184366\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn299\" name\u003D\"_ftnref299\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[299]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184366_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184367\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184367_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E rejected the earlier common law \"theory that the Crown acquired absolute beneficial ownership of land\" deemed to be \u003CI\u003Eterra nullius\u003C/I\u003E (or uninhabited land) as an incident of its acquisition of sovereignty over that land.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184368\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn300\" name\u003D\"_ftnref300\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[300]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184368_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184369\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184369_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E did so in part to ensure the equality of all Australians before the law, in part to cease the perpetuation of injustice that the earlier common law theory had inflicted on the indigenous inhabitants of Australia, and in part to \"bring the law into conformity with Australian history\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184370\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn301\" name\u003D\"_ftnref301\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[301]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184370_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E In describing that history, as Brennan\u00A0J put it:\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184371\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn302\" name\u003D\"_ftnref302\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[302]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184371_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\"The dispossession of the indigenous inhabitants of Australia was not worked by a transfer of beneficial ownership when sovereignty was acquired by the Crown, but by the recurrent exercise of a paramount power to exclude the indigenous inhabitants from their traditional lands as colonial settlement expanded and land was granted to the colonists.\"\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_3102\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref299\" name\u003D\"_ftn299\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[299]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-8\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA native title rights and interests at common law resulting from this Court\u0027s decision in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185620\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184364_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E is rooted in the specific history of the British colonisation and settlement of Australia. As social, economic, political, and demographic pressures evolved in Great Britain over the 18th and 19th centuries, British people (and people from other parts of Europe), either unwillingly (in the case of British convicts) or willingly, made their way to \"the colonies\" including Australia in increasing numbers. As Brennan J observed in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184365\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184365_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E, \"English colonists were, in the eye of the common law, entitled to live under the common law of England which Blackstone described as their \u0027birthright\u0027\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184366\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn299\" name\u003D\"_ftnref299\" [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184367_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E rejected the earlier common law \"theory that the Crown acquired absolute beneficial ownership of land\" deemed to be \u003CI\u003Eterra nullius\u003C/I\u003E (or uninhabited land) as an incident of its acquisition of sovereignty over that land.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184368\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn300\" name\u003D\"_ftnref300\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[300]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184369_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E did so in part to ensure the equality of all Australians before the law, in part to cease the perpetuation of injustice that the earlier common law theory had inflicted on the indigenous inhabitants of Australia, and in part to \"bring the law into conformity with Australian history\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184370\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn301\" name\u003D\"_ftnref301\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[301]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\"The dispossession of the indigenous inhabitants of Australia was not worked by a transfer of beneficial ownership when sovereignty was acquired by the Crown, but by the recurrent exercise of a paramount power to exclude the indigenous inhabitants from their traditional lands as colonial settlement expanded and land was granted to the colonists.\"\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_3102\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"18\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184001\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_sr_1035\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003ESection \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185508\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549/section/8612\"\u003E223(1)\u003C/A\u003E of the \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185509\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENative Title Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184001_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003C/I\u003Edefines the expression \"native title\" or \"native title rights and interests\"\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184002\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn18\" name\u003D\"_ftnref18\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[18]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184002_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E relevantly as follows: \u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\"\u003CI\u003ECommon law rights and interests\u003C/I\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E(1) \u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-10\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003EThe expression \u003CB\u003E\u003CI\u003Enative title\u003C/I\u003E\u003C/B\u003E or \u003CB\u003E\u003CI\u003Enative title rights and interests\u003C/I\u003E\u003C/B\u003E means the communal, group or individual rights and interests of Aboriginal peoples or Torres Strait Islanders in relation to land or waters, where:\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E(a)\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-10\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003Ethe rights and interests are possessed under the traditional laws acknowledged, and the traditional customs observed, by\u00A0the Aboriginal peoples or Torres Strait Islanders; and \u003C/P\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E(b) \u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-10\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003Ethe Aboriginal peoples or Torres Strait Islanders, \u003CI\u003Eby those laws and customs\u003C/I\u003E, \u003CI\u003Ehave a connection with the land or waters\u003C/I\u003E; and\u003C/P\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E(c) \u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-10\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003Ethe rights and interests are recognised by the common law of Australia.\" (emphasis added)\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003CP\u003EThe immediately relevant elements in the definition in\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184003\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E s \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185510\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549/section/8612\"\u003E223(1)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184003_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E of \"native title\" and \"native title rights and interests\" are based on this Court\u0027s decision in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185511\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184004\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland [No 2]\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E,\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn19\" name\u003D\"_ftnref19\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E[19]\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184004_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E and have remained constant since the \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185512\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184005\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENative Title Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184005_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003C/I\u003Ewas passed.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184006\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn20\" name\u003D\"_ftnref20\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[20]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184006_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_1035\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL/LI[159]/P/TEXT()[2]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL/LI[159]/P/I[1]","Para 159","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"159\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_sr_3102\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EThe recognition of native title rights and interests at common law resulting from this Court\u0027s decision in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185620\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184363\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland [No\u00A02]\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn298\" name\u003D\"_ftnref298\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E[298]\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184363_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E and subsequently under the \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185621\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184364\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENative Title Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184364_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E is rooted in the specific history of the British colonisation and settlement of Australia. As social, economic, political, and demographic pressures evolved in Great Britain over the 18th and 19th centuries, British people (and people from other parts of Europe), either unwillingly (in the case of British convicts) or willingly, made their way to \"the colonies\" including Australia in increasing numbers. As Brennan J observed in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184365\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184365_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E, \"English colonists were, in the eye of the common law, entitled to live under the common law of England which Blackstone described as their \u0027birthright\u0027\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184366\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn299\" name\u003D\"_ftnref299\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[299]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184366_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184367\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184367_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E rejected the earlier common law \"theory that the Crown acquired absolute beneficial ownership of land\" deemed to be \u003CI\u003Eterra nullius\u003C/I\u003E (or uninhabited land) as an incident of its acquisition of sovereignty over that land.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184368\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn300\" name\u003D\"_ftnref300\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[300]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184368_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184369\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184369_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E did so in part to ensure the equality of all Australians before the law, in part to cease the perpetuation of injustice that the earlier common law theory had inflicted on the indigenous inhabitants of Australia, and in part to \"bring the law into conformity with Australian history\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184370\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn301\" name\u003D\"_ftnref301\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[301]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184370_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E In describing that history, as Brennan\u00A0J put it:\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184371\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn302\" name\u003D\"_ftnref302\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[302]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184371_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\"The dispossession of the indigenous inhabitants of Australia was not worked by a transfer of beneficial ownership when sovereignty was acquired by the Crown, but by the recurrent exercise of a paramount power to exclude the indigenous inhabitants from their traditional lands as colonial settlement expanded and land was granted to the colonists.\"\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_3102\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[622]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"166\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_sr_3078\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EThis reflects Brennan\u00A0J\u0027s observation in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184382\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184382_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E that:\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184383\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn310\" name\u003D\"_ftnref310\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[310]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184383_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\"Of course in time the laws and customs of any people will change and the rights and interests of the members of the people among themselves will change too. But so long as the people remain as an identifiable community, the members of whom are identified by one another as members of that community living under its laws and customs, \u003CI\u003Ethe communal native title survives\u003C/I\u003E to be enjoyed by the members \u003CI\u003Eaccording to the rights and interests to which they are respectively entitled under the traditionally based laws and customs, as currently acknowledged and observed\u003C/I\u003E.\"\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_3078\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E(1992) 175 CLR 1\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140600\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E61\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA added).\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[604]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta
