native title rights and interests at common law resulting from this Court\u0027s decision in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185620\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184363\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo v Queensland [No\u00A02]\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn298\" name\u003D\"_ftnref298\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[298]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184363_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E and subsequently under the \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185621\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184364\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENative Title Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184364_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E is rooted in the specific history of the British colonisation and settlement of Australia. As social, economic, political, and demographic pressures evolved in Great Britain over the 18th and 19th centuries, British people (and people from other parts of Europe), either unwillingly (in the case of British convicts) or willingly, made their way to \"the colonies\" including Australia in increasing numbers. As Brennan J observed in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184365\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184365_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E, \"English colonists were, in the eye of the common law, entitled to live under the common law of England which Blackstone described as their \u0027birthright\u0027\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184366\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn299\" name\u003D\"_ftnref299\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[299]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184366_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184367\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184367_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E rejected the earlier common law \"theory that the Crown acquired absolute beneficial ownership of land\" deemed to be \u003CI\u003Eterra nullius\u003C/I\u003E (or uninhabited land) as an incident of its acquisition of sovereignty over that land.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184368\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn300\" name\u003D\"_ftnref300\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[300]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184368_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184369\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184369_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E did so in part to ensure the equality of all Australians before the law, in part to cease the perpetuation of injustice that the earlier common law theory had inflicted on the indigenous inhabitants of Australia, and in part to \"bring the law into conformity with Australian history\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184370\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn301\" name\u003D\"_ftnref301\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[301]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184370_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E In describing that history, as Brennan\u00A0J put it:\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184371\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn302\" name\u003D\"_ftnref302\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[302]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184371_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\"The dispossession of the indigenous inhabitants of Australia was not worked by a transfer of beneficial ownership when sovereignty was acquired by the Crown, but by the recurrent exercise of a paramount power to exclude the indigenous inhabitants from their traditional lands as colonial settlement expanded and land was granted to the colonists.\"\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_3102\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref301\" name\u003D\"_ftn301\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[301]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-7\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186362\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184763\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E(1992) 175 CLR 1\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E at \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186363\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/141020\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E58.\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184763_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","STEWARD J.","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL/LI[111]/P[1]/TEXT()[4]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/OL/LI[111]/P[1]/I[1]","Para 111","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"111\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_sr_194\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EAs a matter of principle, it is difficult to accept that a spiritual, religious or cultural connection to particular land or waters, in the absence of any physical connection, could ever satisfy the requirements of\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184256\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E s \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185577\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549/section/8612\"\u003E223(1)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184256_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, concerned as that provision is with the recognition and protection of actual manifest dealings with land or waters. There is nothing in the reasons of Brennan J in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185578\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184257\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland [No 2]\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (\"\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo [No 2]\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E\"),\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn226\" name\u003D\"_ftnref226\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E[226]\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184257_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E upon which\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184258\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E s \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185579\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549/section/8612\"\u003E223(1)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184258_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E is based,\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn227\" name\u003D\"_ftnref227\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[227]\u003C/A\u003E which would support such a conclusion. That is not to deny that particular Indigenous Australians may hold very strong spiritual, religious or cultural links to identifiable land or waters. Writing in 1971, Blackburn\u00A0J certainly considered that the fundamental feature of Indigenous Australians\u0027 relationship to land was that \"whatever else it is, it is a religious relationship\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184259\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn228\" name\u003D\"_ftnref228\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[228]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184259_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E Whether that is still the case today, and whether it is true of all or part of a particular Indigenous Australian group, would be a matter for evidence. But it does not follow that this spiritual, religious or cultural relationship is what the common law recognises. Rather, the common law recognises and protects, amongst other things, the physical manifestations of such a spiritual, religious or cultural connection.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_194\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_61314_sr.t_What is meant by a \u0026#34;connection\u0026#34; with \u0026#34;land or waters\u0026#34;_sr.x_14\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[612]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()[2]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[612]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/I[2]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"162\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_sr_140765\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EAs explained in \u003CI\u003ECommonwealth of Australia\u00A0v\u00A0Yunupingu\u003C/I\u003E:\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184374\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn305\" name\u003D\"_ftnref305\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[305]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184374_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\"Native title is \u0027not an institution of the common law\u0027. That is to say, \u0027native title rights and interests are not created by and do not derive from the common law\u0027. ... \u003C/P\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u0027Native title has its origin in and is given its content by the traditional laws acknowledged by and the traditional customs observed by the indigenous inhabitants of a territory\u0027. \u0027The nature and incidents of native title\u0027 are \u0027ascertained as a matter of fact by reference to those laws and customs\u0027. The underlying existence of the traditional laws and customs ascertained as a matter of fact is accordingly \u0027a \u003CI\u003Enecessary\u003C/I\u003E [but not a sufficient] pre-requisite for native title\u0027.\"\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_140765\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref305\" name\u003D\"_ftn305\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[305]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-7\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186372\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184767\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/1119338\"\u003ECommonwealth of Australia v Yunupingu\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [2025] HCA 6 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186371\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/1119338/section/4039\"\u003E[58]-[59]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184767_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, quoting \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186375\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184768\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo\u00A0v\u00A0Queensland [No 2]\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (1992) 175 CLR 1 at \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186374\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140228\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E59\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184768_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E; \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186379\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184769\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68291\"\u003EThe Commonwealth v Yarmirr\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (2001) 208 CLR 1 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186377\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68291/section/140756\"\u003E51\u003C/A\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186378\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68291/section/140756\"\u003E[48]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184769_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186382\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184770\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo v Queensland [No 2]\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (1992) 175 CLR 1 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186381\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/141020\"\u003E58\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184770_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186386\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184771\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68082\"\u003EFejo v Northern Territory\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (1998) 195 CLR 96 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186384\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68082/section/433\"\u003E128\u003C/A\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186385\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68082/section/433\"\u003E[46]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184771_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E (emphasis in original) (footnotes omitted).\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[462]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"112\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_sr_1137\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EThe foregoing is compelled by the language of\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184260\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E s \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185580\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549/section/8612\"\u003E223(1)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184260_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E. As set out above, at its core,\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184261\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E s \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185581\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549/section/8612\"\u003E223(1)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184261_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E defines \"native title\" (and \"native title rights and interests\") by reference to \"rights and interests\". These need to be \"in relation to\" land or waters. No one has suggested that the \"rights and interests\" could be of a spiritual, religious or cultural nature and nothing else. Rather, and self\u2011evidently, those \"rights and interests\" are, as Gleeson CJ, Gaudron, Gummow and Hayne JJ made clear in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185582\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184262\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68337\"\u003EWard\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184262_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E, essentially \"usufructuary in nature\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184263\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn229\" name\u003D\"_ftnref229\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[229]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184263_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E That observation coheres with the reasoning of Brennan\u00A0J in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184264\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No 2]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184264_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E, where his Honour said the following:\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184265\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn230\" name\u003D\"_ftnref230\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[230]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184265_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\"[N]ative title, being recognized by the common law (though not as a common law tenure), may be protected by such legal or equitable remedies as are appropriate to the particular rights and interests established by the evidence, whether proprietary or personal \u003CI\u003Eand usufructuary in nature \u003C/I\u003Eand whether possessed by a community, a group or an individual\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_1137\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref230\" name\u003D\"_ftn230\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[230]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-7\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186193\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184692\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E(1992) 175 CLR 1\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E at \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186194\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140600\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E61\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E per Brennan J\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184692_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (emphasis added).\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[602]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"159\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_sr_3102\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EThe recognition of native title rights and interests at common law resulting from this Court\u0027s decision in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185620\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184363\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo v Queensland [No\u00A02]\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn298\" name\u003D\"_ftnref298\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[298]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184363_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E and subsequently under the \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185621\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184364\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENative Title Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184364_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E is rooted in the specific history of the British colonisation and settlement of Australia. As social, economic, political, and demographic pressures evolved in Great Britain over the 18th and 19th centuries, British people (and people from other parts of Europe), either unwillingly (in the case of British convicts) or willingly, made their way to \"the colonies\" including Australia in increasing numbers. As Brennan J observed in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184365\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184365_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E, \"English colonists were, in the eye of the common law, entitled to live under the common law of England which Blackstone described as their \u0027birthright\u0027\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184366\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn299\" name\u003D\"_ftnref299\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[299]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184366_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184367\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184367_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E rejected the earlier common law \"theory that the Crown acquired absolute beneficial ownership of land\" deemed to be \u003CI\u003Eterra nullius\u003C/I\u003E (or uninhabited land) as an incident of its acquisition of sovereignty over that land.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184368\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn300\" name\u003D\"_ftnref300\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[300]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184368_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184369\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184369_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E did so in part to ensure the equality of all Australians before the law, in part to cease the perpetuation of injustice that the earlier common law theory had inflicted on the indigenous inhabitants of Australia, and in part to \"bring the law into conformity with Australian history\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184370\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn301\" name\u003D\"_ftnref301\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[301]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184370_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E In describing that history, as Brennan\u00A0J put it:\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184371\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn302\" name\u003D\"_ftnref302\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[302]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184371_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\"The dispossession of the indigenous inhabitants of Australia was not worked by a transfer of beneficial ownership when sovereignty was acquired by the Crown, but by the recurrent exercise of a paramount power to exclude the indigenous inhabitants from their traditional lands as colonial settlement expanded and land was granted to the colonists.\"\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_3102\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref300\" name\u003D\"_ftn300\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[300]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-7\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186360\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184762\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E(1992) 175 CLR 1\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E at \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186361\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/141020\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E58.\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184762_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[612]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()[4]","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[612]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/I[4]","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"162\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_sr_140765\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EAs explained in \u003CI\u003ECommonwealth of Australia\u00A0v\u00A0Yunupingu\u003C/I\u003E:\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184374\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn305\" name\u003D\"_ftnref305\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[305]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184374_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\"Native title is \u0027not an institution of the common law\u0027. That is to say, \u0027native title rights and interests are not created by and do not derive from the common law\u0027. ... \u003C/P\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u0027Native title has its origin in and is given its content by the traditional laws acknowledged by and the traditional customs observed by the indigenous inhabitants of a territory\u0027. \u0027The nature and incidents of native title\u0027 are \u0027ascertained as a matter of fact by reference to those laws and customs\u0027. The underlying existence of the traditional laws and customs ascertained as a matter of fact is accordingly \u0027a \u003CI\u003Enecessary\u003C/I\u003E [but not a sufficient] pre-requisite for native title\u0027.\"\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_140765\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref305\" name\u003D\"_ftn305\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[305]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-7\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186372\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184767\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/1119338\"\u003ECommonwealth of Australia v Yunupingu\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E [2025] HCA 6 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186371\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/1119338/section/4039\"\u003E[58]-[59]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184767_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, quoting \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186375\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184768\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo\u00A0v\u00A0Queensland [No 2]\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (1992) 175 CLR 1 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186374\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/140228\"\u003E59\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184768_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186379\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184769\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68291\"\u003EThe Commonwealth v Yarmirr\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (2001) 208 CLR 1 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186377\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68291/section/140756\"\u003E51\u003C/A\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186378\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68291/section/140756\"\u003E[48]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184769_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E; \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186382\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184770\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003EMabo v Queensland [No 2]\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E (1992) 175 CLR 1 at \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186381\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683/section/141020\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E58\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184770_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E; \u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186386\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184771\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68082\"\u003EFejo v Northern Territory\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (1998) 195 CLR 96 at \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186384\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68082/section/433\"\u003E128\u003C/A\u003E \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186385\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/68082/section/433\"\u003E[46]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184771_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E (emphasis in original) (footnotes omitted).\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[454]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"111\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_sr_194\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EAs a matter of principle, it is difficult to accept that a spiritual, religious or cultural connection to particular land or waters, in the absence of any physical connection, could ever satisfy the requirements of\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184256\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E s \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185577\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549/section/8612\"\u003E223(1)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184256_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E, concerned as that provision is with the recognition and protection of actual manifest dealings with land or waters. There is nothing in the reasons of Brennan J in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185578\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184257\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo v Queensland [No 2]\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E (\"\u003CI\u003EMabo [No 2]\u003C/I\u003E\"),\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn226\" name\u003D\"_ftnref226\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[226]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184257_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E upon which\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184258\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E s \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185579\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549/section/8612\"\u003E223(1)\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184258_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E is based,\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn227\" name\u003D\"_ftnref227\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[227]\u003C/A\u003E which would support such a conclusion. That is not to deny that particular Indigenous Australians may hold very strong spiritual, religious or cultural links to identifiable land or waters. Writing in 1971, Blackburn\u00A0J certainly considered that the fundamental feature of Indigenous Australians\u0027 relationship to land was that \"whatever else it is, it is a religious relationship\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184259\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn228\" name\u003D\"_ftnref228\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[228]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184259_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E Whether that is still the case today, and whether it is true of all or part of a particular Indigenous Australian group, would be a matter for evidence. But it does not follow that this spiritual, religious or cultural relationship is what the common law recognises. Rather, the common law recognises and protects, amongst other things, the physical manifestations of such a spiritual, religious or cultural connection.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_194\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_61314_sr.t_What is meant by a \u0026#34;connection\u0026#34; with \u0026#34;land or waters\u0026#34;_sr.x_14\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref226\" name\u003D\"_ftn226\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[226]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-8\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186181\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184688\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E(1992) 175 CLR 1\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184688_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E.\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[598]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"159\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_sr_3102\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EThe recognition of native title rights and interests at common law resulting from this Court\u0027s decision in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185620\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184363\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003EMabo v Queensland [No\u00A02]\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn298\" name\u003D\"_ftnref298\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[298]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184363_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E and subsequently under the \u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838185621\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184364\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/218549\"\u003ENative Title Act\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184364_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E is rooted in the specific history of the British colonisation and settlement of Australia. As social, economic, political, and demographic pressures evolved in Great Britain over the 18th and 19th centuries, British people (and people from other parts of Europe), either unwillingly (in the case of British convicts) or willingly, made their way to \"the colonies\" including Australia in increasing numbers. As Brennan J observed in \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184365\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184365_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E, \"English colonists were, in the eye of the common law, entitled to live under the common law of England which Blackstone described as their \u0027birthright\u0027\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184366\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn299\" name\u003D\"_ftnref299\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[299]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184366_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184367\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184367_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E rejected the earlier common law \"theory that the Crown acquired absolute beneficial ownership of land\" deemed to be \u003CI\u003Eterra nullius\u003C/I\u003E (or uninhabited land) as an incident of its acquisition of sovereignty over that land.\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184368\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn300\" name\u003D\"_ftnref300\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[300]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184368_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E \u003CI\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184369\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EMabo [No\u00A02]\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184369_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/I\u003E did so in part to ensure the equality of all Australians before the law, in part to cease the perpetuation of injustice that the earlier common law theory had inflicted on the indigenous inhabitants of Australia, and in part to \"bring the law into conformity with Australian history\".\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184370\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn301\" name\u003D\"_ftnref301\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[301]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184370_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E In describing that history, as Brennan\u00A0J put it:\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184371\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn302\" name\u003D\"_ftnref302\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[302]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184371_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003CBLOCKQUOTE class\u003D\"jade-noVerticalMargins jade-tabIndent\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\"The dispossession of the indigenous inhabitants of Australia was not worked by a transfer of beneficial ownership when sovereignty was acquired by the Crown, but by the recurrent exercise of a paramount power to exclude the indigenous inhabitants from their traditional lands as colonial settlement expanded and land was granted to the colonists.\"\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_e_t_sr_3102\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/BLOCKQUOTE\u003E\u003C/LI\u003E\u003C/OL\u003E\u003Chr\u003E\u003Cdiv class\u003D\u0027extract-via\u0027\u003E\u003Ci\u003Evia\u003C/i\u003E\u003CP class\u003D\"jade-outdent\"\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftnref298\" name\u003D\"_ftn298\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[298]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-ts-8\"\u003E\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_cc_838186357\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184760\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA class\u003D\"bn_wrapped_span jade-out-article\" href\u003D\"https://jade.io/article/67683\"\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E(1992) 175 CLR 1\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184760_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CSPAN class\u003D\"jade-incoming-citation\"\u003E.\u003C/SPAN\u003E\u003C/P\u003E\u003C/div\u003E\u003C/body\u003E\u003C/html\u003E","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[596]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/TEXT()","/HTML/BODY/DIV/BLOCKQUOTE[596]/BLOCKQUOTE/DIV/P/I","\u003Chtml\u003E\u003Chead\u003E\u003Cmeta http-equiv\u003D\u0027Content-Type\u0027 content\u003D\"text/html; charset\u003DUTF-8\"\u003E\u003Ctitle\u003EContent extract - BarNet Jade\u003C/title\u003E\u003C/head\u003E\u003Cbody\u003E\u003COL\u003E\u003CLI value\u003D\"157\"\u003E\u003CP\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_sr_1745\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003EAs will be explained, the case confronting the courts below involved the operation of the \"tide of history\"\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184362\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA href\u003D\"#_ftn297\" name\u003D\"_ftnref297\" title\u003D\"\"\u003E[297]\u003C/A\u003E\u003CA id\u003D\"bnj_a_1127773_ct_583184362_e_t\"\u003E\u003C/A\u003E in which there were undoubted significant effects on the Arabana People and the Walka Wani Group from European settlement, but also, based on the primary judge\u0027s unchallenged findings, significant effects on both the Arabana People and the Walka Wani Group from environmental pressures before and after European settlement\u00A0—\u00A0particularly drought\u00A0—\u00A0causing a substantial southward migration of the ancestors of members of the Walka Wani Group towards and into the traditional country of the Arabana People. \u003CA
